가능\r","39659-\r","--","39687-\r","39688-## Related\r","39689:- [[projects/CTX/research/20260328-ctx-downstream-eval-nemotron-final|20260328-ctx-downstream-eval-nemotron-final]]\r","39690-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260329-ctx-corrected-results-summary|20260329-ctx-corrected-results-summary]]\r","39691-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260328-ctx-downstream-minimax-eval|20260328-ctx-downstream-minimax-eval]]\r","--","39692-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260328-ctx-downstream-nemotron-eval|20260328-ctx-downstream-nemotron-eval]]\r","--","39693-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260328-ctx-downstream-eval-complete|20260328-ctx-downstream-eval-complete]]\r","--","39694-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260328-ctx-real-codebase-g2-eval|20260328-ctx-real-codebase-g2-eval]]\r","--","39695-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260327-ctx-downstream-eval|20260327-ctx-downstream-eval]]\r","--","39696-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260328-ctx-downstream-nemotron-eval-v2|20260328-ctx-downstream-nemotron-eval-v2]]","--","39697-</file>","--","39698-","--","39702-\r","39703-## Original Question\r","39704:Rigorous, web-grounded academic critique of CTX retrieval system's claimed results across: synthetic benchmark validity, external generalization, downstream LLM eval, TES metric, and improvement prioritization.\r","39705-\r","39706----\r","--","39712-[FACT-2] CodeRAG-Bench (NAACL 2025 Findings, arXiv:2406.14497): BM25 achieves avg NDCG@10=57.7 on code RAG tasks; top dense models (SFR-Mistral) reach 67.0. Dense retrievers outperform BM25 on most code tasks. Source: https://arxiv.org/html/2406.14497v2\r","39713-\r","39714:[FACT-3] LocAgent (ACL 2025, arXiv:2503.09089): Graph-guided LLM agents achieve 92.7% file-level localization accuracy, 94.16% Acc@5 on SWE-Bench-Lite. Uses directed heterogeneous graph (files, classes, functions, imports, invocations) + multi-hop LLM traversal (SearchEntity/TraverseGraph/RetrieveEntity tools). 86% cost reduction vs. proprietary models. Source: https://aclanthology.org/2025.acl-long.426/\r","39715-\r","39716-[FACT-4] RAG faithfulness tug-of-war (Xu et al. 2024, arXiv:2404.10198): RAG with correct context improves concordance from 34.7% → 94%. Prior-adherence inverse correlation: slope -0.23 average across 6 domains (range: -0.10 news to -0.45 date questions). LLMs adopt incorrect retrieved content when parametric confidence is low. Source: https://arxiv.org/html/2404.10198v1/\r","--","39717-\r","39718-[FACT-5] BM25 known failure modes: lexical mismatch affects ~15-20% of queries; IDF computation in small/distributed corpora produces unstable scores; domain-specific terms appearing in many documents get low IDF weight — directly explaining CTX's finding that BM25Okapi underperforms TF-only BM25 on 29-doc domain corpus. Source: https://www.systemoverflow.com/learn/search-ranking/ranking-algorithms/bm25-failure-modes-and-production-mitigations\r","--","39719-\r","39720-[FACT-6] Hybrid retrieval (BM25 + dense): consistently outperforms either standalone by 3-8% NDCG@10 in BEIR/CoIR studies. ColBERT+BM25 tuning achieves relative gains up to +0.93pp. BMX (entropy-weighted BM25 variant) and LexBoost improve over vanilla BM25 without semantic embeddings. Source: https://infiniflow.org/blog/best-hybrid-search-solution\r","--","39721-\r","39722-[FACT-7] BEIR benchmark (Thakur et al. 2021/2024): BM25 remains top performer across diverse zero-shot tasks post-cleaning. Length-based denoising improved neural retrievers by +0.52 nDCG@10 (TAS-B) but BM25 remains competitive. BEIR uses nDCG@10 as primary metric, not Recall@k. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2104.08663\r","--","39723-\r","39724-[FACT-8] RAG over-anchoring and context distraction: \"increasing retrieved passages does not consistently improve performance.\" Models are \"prone to distraction\" when irrelevant context is mixed with relevant. Up to 57% of LLM citations are post-rationalized. Source: ICLR 2025, https://proceedings.iclr.cc/paper_files/paper/2025/file/5df5b1f121c915d8bdd00db6aac20827-Paper-Conference.pdf\r","--","39725-\r","39726-[FACT-9] TES (Token Efficiency Score = Recall@5 / ln(1+files_loaded)): No prior work in BEIR, CoIR, CodeRAG-Bench, or BEIR uses this metric formulation. Standard efficiency-recall tradeoffs in IR are measured via latency@k, FLOP counts, or P@k/R@k curves. The closest recognized concept is Context Precision in RAGAS. Source: https://www.confident-ai.com/blog/rag-evaluation-metrics-answer-relevancy-faithfulness-and-more\r","--","39727-\r","39728:[FACT-10] CTX internal finding (project CLAUDE.md): CosQA official evaluation (N=500) produced NDCG@10=0.1223, Recall@10=0.232 — far below dense model baselines on the same task. This external result was not highlighted in the paper abstract.\r","39729-\r","39730----\r","--","39735-\r","39736-**Finding 1: The 29-doc synthetic benchmark is not a valid IR benchmark by 2025 standards.**\r","39737:BEIR uses 18 datasets with thousands to millions of documents per task. CoIR's smallest task still has hundreds of documents. A 29-document corpus with 87 queries derived from the same project tests lookup skill, not retrieval generalization. The R@3=0.862 result is not surprising — with 29 documents, a heading-match heuristic will trivially achieve high recall for heading-structured queries. No meaningful comparison to BEIR or CoIR baselines is possible.\r","39738-\r","39739-**Finding 2: The external generalization gap (R@5: 0.862 → 0.495 → CosQA 0.232) is catastrophic.**\r","--","39740:The project's own CosQA official evaluation (N=500) yielded Recall@10=0.232, NDCG@10=0.1223. On the CTX-internal COIR-CodeSearchNet subset (N=100), the claimed R@5=0.495 with BM25 baseline=0.980. This means CTX performs at ~50% of vanilla BM25 on external corpora. The heuristic trigger-classification layer is not just failing to help — it is actively harmful (below BM25 sub-component performance). Rule-based systems overfitting to their development codebase is a documented IR problem, but the CTX gap is among the worst published.\r","39741-\r","39742-**Finding 3: LocAgent (ACL 2025) is the strongest counter-evidence to CTX's architecture.**\r","--","39743:LocAgent achieves 92.7% file-level localization and 94.16% Acc@5 on SWE-Bench-Lite — precisely the task type CTX targets — using graph-guided multi-hop LLM traversal. This is not an aspirational future direction; it is a published, reproducible, open-source system that directly supersedes CTX's import-BFS approach. CTX's 3-hop BFS without LLM guidance cannot match multi-hop LLM agent traversal on complex dependency chains.\r","39744-\r","39745-**Finding 4: The G1 downstream result (1.000 WITH, 0.219 WITHOUT) is a trivial finding.**\r","--","39746:G1 tests whether persistent_memory injected into context enables recall. When the answer is verbatim in the prompt, Recall=1.000 is guaranteed by any competent LLM. The WITHOUT=0.219 measures the LLM's ability to recall a specific session's data from parametric memory only — which no LLM is designed to do. This is a \"context vs. no-context\" ablation, not a retrieval quality evaluation. It proves that context injection works, not that CTX's retrieval is good.\r","39747-\r","39748-**Finding 5: Over-anchoring (20%) is consistent with literature but mechanistically mislabeled.**\r","--","39749:Xu et al. (2024) document the prior-adherence tug-of-war with domain-dependent slopes (-0.10 to -0.45). CTX's 20% over-anchoring frequency is within the literature range. However, CTX labels the phenomenon as \"creative suppression\" — this is distinct from knowledge-conflict anchoring. The two mechanisms are: (a) LLM adopts incorrect retrieved facts over correct parametric knowledge (Xu et al.), and (b) LLM anchors on code structure and fails to propose novel implementations. The paper should distinguish these clearly. \"Over-anchoring\" as a unified concept conflates two different failure modes.\r","39750-\r","39751----\r","--","39757-\r","39758-**[OVERCONFIDENT] G2 \"+0.688 normalized\" is methodologically undefined.**\r","39759:The raw G2 delta is +0.200 (0.350 vs 0.150). The paper reports \"+0.688 normalized.\" The normalization formula is not described anywhere in the accessible project documentation. This smells like a post-hoc rescaling applied to make a modest improvement appear larger. Reviewers will request the raw numbers and the normalization specification.\r","39760-\r","39761-**[MISSING] No statistical significance on primary synthetic benchmark.**\r","--","39762:With n=87 queries and 29 documents, no p-values or confidence intervals are reported for the main R@3=0.862 vs BM25=0.724 comparison (Δ=0.138). The project has Bootstrap CIs for some experiments (final_report_v10.md) but not for the synthetic 29-doc benchmark. This is the most-cited number and it has no significance test.\r","39763-\r","39764-**[MISSING] Trigger classifier accuracy (60.2%) is a silent killer.**\r","--","39765:benchmarks/results/trigger_accuracy.md reports 60.2% classifier accuracy with SEMANTIC_CONCEPT F1=0.15. If the trigger routing system is wrong 40% of the time, any claimed improvement over BM25 in non-heading-match queries is confounded by misrouting. The synthetic benchmark's R@3=0.862 is achieved with a 60.2% accurate router — the heading_paraphrase=1.000 result masks the router failures on other query types.\r","39766-\r","39767-**[CONFLICT] External R@5=0.495 contradicts the claim that CTX \"uses BM25 as a sub-component.\"**\r","--","39776-\r","39777-**What is genuinely valid in CTX's results:**\r","39778:1. The heading-match heuristic produces real, measurable improvements for structured documentation queries in small, known corpora. This is a legitimate finding.\r","39779-2. The over-anchoring 20% observation is a real phenomenon worth reporting, provided it distinguishes knowledge-conflict anchoring from creative-suppression anchoring.\r","39780-3. TES captures a real engineering concern (context window efficiency) that standard IR metrics miss. It could be valuable if defined formally and compared to Context Precision metrics in RAGAS.\r","--","39781-4. The IMPLICIT_CONTEXT via import BFS is a genuine contribution for dependency-following queries on known codebases (R@5 improvement of 362-441% from the import graph approach per the project's own benchmark).\r","--","39782-\r","39783-**What is methodologically weak or misleading:**\r","--","39786-3. G2 \"+0.688 normalized\" must be either replaced with the raw +0.200 or fully justified.\r","39787-4. The Nemotron comparison should be removed or replaced with publicly available baselines (BM25, BM25+, Contriever, UniXcoder from CoIR).\r","39788:5. The external R@5=0.495 vs BM25=0.980 gap must be prominently disclosed, not buried.\r","39789-\r","39790----\r","--","39793-\r","39794-### Results That Are Strong\r","39795:- **Heading-paraphrase R@3=1.000 on structured docs**: Genuine, reproducible, architecturally explainable.\r","39796-- **IMPLICIT_CONTEXT import BFS improvement (+362-441%)**: Real, novel, and not replicated by other tools (confirmed by head-to-head vs mcp__code-search__).\r","39797-- **Over-anchoring identification**: Directionally correct, consistent with literature.\r","--","39798-\r","--","39799-### Results That Are Weak\r","39800-- **R@3=0.862 synthetic**: Only meaningful within the 29-doc in-distribution setting. Not publishable as a retrieval result.\r","39801:- **Keyword R@3=0.724 (tied with BM25)**: No improvement demonstrated; the trigger-routing system adds complexity without benefit for keyword queries.\r","39802-- **Trigger classifier accuracy=60.2%**: The routing layer is the system's most critical component and is performing below random on SEMANTIC queries (F1=0.15). This undermines all claims that depend on correct routing.\r","39803-\r","--","39804-### Results That Are Misleading\r","39805:- **G1=1.000 downstream**: Trivial context-in-prompt measurement.\r","39806-- **G2 \"+0.688 normalized\"**: Undefined normalization of a raw +0.200 delta.\r","39807-- **CosQA NDCG@10=0.1223**: Published in internal reports but not highlighted as the primary external validity measure it should be.\r","--","39808:- **Nemotron-Cascade-2 comparison**: Non-reproducible baseline.\r","39809-\r","39810----\r","--","39811-\r","39812:## Top 3 Improvement Recommendations (by ROI)\r","39813-\r","39814-### Recommendation 1: Fix the Trigger Classifier (Immediate, Highest ROI)\r","--","39815:**Current problem**: 60.2% accuracy, SEMANTIC F1=0.15.\r","39816-**Why it matters**: Every claim about CTX outperforming BM25 is confounded by 40% misrouting. Improving the classifier to 85%+ accuracy would improve all downstream metrics and validate the routing architecture.\r","39817-**Approach**: Replace heuristic classifier with a fine-tuned small model (DistilBERT or CodeBERT) on a manually labeled dataset of 500+ queries. The trigger taxonomy is well-defined; supervised classification on this taxonomy would be straightforward.\r","--","39818-**Expected impact**: Based on ablation results in the project, correct routing accounts for most of the heading_paraphrase=1.000 performance. Extending correct routing to SEMANTIC and IMPLICIT queries could push overall R@3 from 0.862 to 0.90+ on the synthetic benchmark and more importantly improve external R@5.\r","--","39819-\r","--","39820-### Recommendation 2: Replace Heuristic-Only External Retrieval with Hybrid BM25+Dense (High ROI)\r","--","39821:**Current problem**: External R@5=0.495 (vs BM25=0.980). CTX's heuristics are overriding BM25 with wrong answers on unseen codebases.\r","39822-**Literature basis**: Hybrid BM25+dense consistently outperforms either standalone by 3-8% NDCG@10 (BEIR, CoIR literature). ColBERT+BM25 tuning achieves +0.93pp relative gain. For external codebases, a dense model (UniXcoder or Voyage-Code-002) combined with BM25 would close the gap from 0.495 toward BM25's 0.980.\r","39823-**Approach**: For EXTERNAL_CODEBASE queries (detected when a query does not match the indexed known codebase), fall back to hybrid BM25+UniXcoder instead of heuristic matching. This is a targeted improvement for the generalization gap without touching the in-distribution performance.\r","--","39824-**Expected impact**: External R@5 from 0.495 toward 0.800+. This is the most critical gap for any practical deployment claim.\r","39825-\r","--","39826-### Recommendation 3: Upgrade Import BFS to LocAgent-style Graph Traversal (Medium-term, Publication-critical)\r","39827:**Current problem**: 2-3 hop BFS on import graph without LLM guidance; cannot handle complex multi-hop reasoning (CTX weakness P4).\r","39828-**Literature basis**: LocAgent (ACL 2025) achieves 94.16% Acc@5 on SWE-Bench-Lite using LLM-guided graph traversal with the same data structure CTX already has (import graph). The core difference is LLM-guided node selection vs. BFS.\r","39829-**Approach**: Integrate an LLM oracle for graph traversal decisions (which nodes to expand next) using the existing import graph. This aligns CTX with LocAgent's architecture and makes it directly comparable to state-of-the-art.\r","--","39830-**Expected impact**: File localization accuracy from ~50% (estimated on external) to potentially 80-90%+ on complex dependency queries. Also provides a defensible comparison target for paper submission (ACL/EMNLP 2026 or EMNLP 2025 workshop).\r","39831-\r","--","39832----\r","--","39838-- [How faithful are RAG models? Tug-of-war between RAG and LLMs' internal prior (2024)](https://arxiv.org/html/2404.10198v1/)\r","39839-- [BEIR: A Heterogeneous Benchmark for Zero-shot IR (NeurIPS 2021)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2104.08663)\r","39840:- [BM25 Failure Modes and Production Note: deletion decisions (OBSOLETE) can still be valid context.\r","39984-\r","39985-IP@7 → \"Is the newest iteration of recurring topics injected?\"\r","--","39986:       Relevant in spiral projects; becomes meaningful with n≥50.\r","39987-\r","39988-Validity@5 → \"Are the injected decisions factually correct for current state?\"\r","--","39990-\r","39991-Downstream LLM delta → \"Does the injection actually change LLM behavior?\"\r","39992:                       The ultimate eval. Without this, all other metrics are proxies.\r","39993-```\r","39994-\r","--","40047-## Script\r","40048-\r","40049:`benchmarks/eval/g1_final_eval.py` — fully automated, runs on any git When a rapidly\r","40120:versioning project commits many version bumps, spiral topic decisions get pushed\r","40121-below the 7-cap by structurally different (but more recent) commits.\r","40122-\r","--","40126-and \"command hook + additionalContext for SOTA G1\" (OBSOLETE — hook restructured).\r","40127-\r","40128:**However**: These are highly VALID decisions for understanding current project direction.\r","40129-\"Old CTX remnants removed\" is essential context for knowing WHY current architecture\r","40130-exists. Most recurring topics\r","40148:span 30-100 commits (10+ days in active projects).\r","40149-\r","40150-### Finding 5: SpiralDepth Mean = 1.14 — Projects Are Primarily Layer 0-1\r","--","40151-\r","40152-Injected decisions are mostly Layer 0 (introduction) and Layer 1 (problem identification).\r","--","40153-Layer 2-3 commits (solutions, integrations) are rarer — they tend to occur after longer\r","40154-development spirals that fall outside the n=20 window.\r","--","40161-|--------|-----------|-----------|---------------|\r","40162-| **IP@7** | Fraction of spiral clusters where newest iteration in injected top-7 | Full auto (git) | 0.000 (n=20) |\r","40163:| **DA@7** | Fraction of injected decisions with active code files in HEAD | git-oracle proxy | 0.000–1.000 (varies) |\r","40164-| **SD** | Mean spiral depth layer (0=intro, 1=problem, 2=solution, 3=integration) | Full auto (keywords) | 1.14 avg |\r","40165-| **Spiral Miss Rate** | % of clusters where newest is in tail (not injected) | Full auto | 100% (n=20) |\r","--","40166-| **Spiral Noise Rate** | % of clusters where ALL iterations injected (redundant) | Full auto | 0% |\r","--","40171-\r","40172-1. **n=20 too small**: Only 2 spiral clusters detected (insufficient for statistical conclusions)\r","40173:2. **DA@7 proxy flaw**: Deletion decisions marked OBSOLETE despite being valid context\r","40174-3. **Topic clustering by file**: Different decisions on same file ≠ same logical topic\r","40175-   (e.g., unrelated bug fix + feature add to same file = false spiral)\r","--","40181-## Implications for G1 Design\r","40182-\r","40183:### Immediate improvement: topic-aware reranking in get_git_decisions()\r","40184-\r","40185-Current behavior: top-7 by recency (topic-blind)\r","--","40186:Proposed: deduplicate by topic cluster → inject newest-per-topic before others\r","40187-\r","40188-```python\r","--","40204-### Increase n or use topic-window [[projects/CTX/research/20260326-ctx-vs-sota-comparison|20260326-ctx-vs-sota-comparison]]","--","41831-</file>","--","41832-","--","41837-**Framework**: G1 Long-Term Memory Evaluation v1.0\r","41838-**Model**: MiniMax M2.5 (Anthropic-compatible)\r","41839:**Dataset**: CTX project, 59 decision commits, 59 Type-1 QA pairs\r","41840-\r","41841----\r","--","41844-\r","41845-Full 7-baseline evaluation completed on 59 QA pairs.\r","41846:**Key finding**: BM25 query-aware retrieval (0.881 recall, ~174 tokens) dramatically outperforms the current CTX git-memory proactive injection (0.169 recall, ~204 tokens), while using 17.5x less context than full dump.\r","41847-\r","41848----\r","--","41857-| full_dump | 0.712 | ~12,186 chars | ~3,046 | 1.0x (baseline) | Full git log |\r","41858-| dense_embedding | 0.644 | ~758 chars | ~190 | 16.1x smaller | Query-aware RAG |\r","41859:| g1_raw | 0.305 | ~1,876 chars | ~469 | 6.5x smaller | Proactive (sim) |\r","41860-| **git_memory_real** | **0.169** | ~816 chars | ~204 | 14.9x smaller | **CTX current** |\r","41861-| g1_filtered | 0.169 | ~726 chars | ~182 | 16.8x smaller | Proactive (sim, filtered) |\r","--","41862-| no_ctx | 0.000 | 0 chars | 0 | N/A | No context |\r","41863-\r","--","41890-- Full dump includes irrelevant noise (merge commits, formatting changes, etc.)\r","41891-\r","41892:### Finding 2: CTX git_memory_real (proactive injection) recall = 0.169\r","41893-\r","41894-The current CTX git-memory hook achieves only **16.9% recall** on Type-1 timestamp queries.\r","--","41910-**0-7d recall = 0.222**: Even for very recent commits (within 7 days), git_memory_real only achieves 22.2% recall. The DECISION_CAP=7 and topic deduplication aggressively prunes context.\r","41911-\r","41912:**7-30d recall = 0.000**: Zero recall for commits 7-30 days old. These commits are simply not included in the proactive injection window (n=30 recent commits, focusing on last ~7 days of activity).\r","41913-\r","41914-### Finding 3: Dense Embedding is competitive (0.644 recall, 16x smaller)\r","--","41922-Dense embedding is **3.8x worse than BM25** for this task. This aligns with the COIR benchmark finding that BM25 often outperforms dense retrieval for code and structured text (commit messages follow predictable patterns that BM25 exploits).\r","41923-\r","41924:### Finding 4: Proactive injection catastrophically fails on 7-30d queries\r","41925-\r","41926-**All proactive baselines (g1_raw, g1_filtered, git_memory_real) achieve 0.000 recall on 7-30d queries.**\r","--","41927-\r","41928-This is the fundamental limitation of proactive/query-agnostic injection:\r","--","41929-- Without knowing what the user will ask, the system can only include recent context\r","41930-- 7-30 day old commits are outside the injection window (n=20-30 decisions)\r","--","41933-### Finding 5: full_dump (oracle) ceiling is 71.2%, not 100%\r","41934-\r","41935:Even with full git log (n=100 commits), recall is only 71.2%. This establishes the upper bound for context-injection approaches without corpus pre-processing.\r","41936-\r","41937-The 28.8% gap between full_dump and BM25 is explained by:\r","--","41938:1. Many decision commits fall outside the n=100 window (project history > 100 commits)\r","41939-2. BM25 corpus contains all 59 extracted decisions regardless of commit position\r","41940-\r","--","41947-Session start → git log → _is_decision() filter → DECISION_CAP=7 dedup → inject\r","41948-  Recall: 0.169 (16.9%)\r","41949:  Token cost: ~204 tokens\r","41950-```\r","41951-\r","--","41953-```\r","41954-Session start → git log → extract all decisions (full corpus)\r","41955:User prompt → BM25 query → top-k relevant decisions → inject\r","41956-  Recall: 0.881 (88.1%)\r","41957-  Token cost: ~174 tokens (17.5x smaller than oracle)\r","--","41958-```\r","41959-\r","--","41960:This is a **5.2x improvement in recall at similar token cost**.\r","41961-\r","41962-The key change: shift G1 from **proactive/session-start** injection to **reactive/query-time** retrieval.\r","--","41963-\r","41964----\r","--","41973-| Query type | \"When did we implement X?\" | Cross-session recall | Long-document recall |\r","41974-| Best method | BM25 (0.881) | Summarization + Retrieval | RAG with recency |\r","41975:| Proactive baseline | 0.169 | — | — |\r","41976-\r","41977-**Key parallel**: LongMemEval shows RAG-based methods consistently outperform proactive summarization for long-horizon recall — consistent with this finding.\r","--","41978-\r","41979-### BM25 vs Dense: Why BM25 Wins on Commit Messages\r","--","42012-```\r","42013-\r","42014:**Pareto-optimal frontiers**:\r","42015-- BM25: 0.881 recall at 174 tokens — dominates all other methods\r","42016-- Dense: 0.644 recall at 190 tokens — dominated by BM25 but lower token cost vs full_dump\r","--","42017-\r","42018----\r","--","42022-### Immediate Action: Upgrade G1 to BM25 Retrieval\r","42023-\r","42024:**Expected improvement**: 0.169 → 0.881 recall (+5.2x)\r","42025-**Token cost**: Similar (~174 vs ~204 tokens)\r","42026-\r","--","42027-Implementation:\r","--","42028-1. At session start: extract all decision commits from git log (no cap)\r","42029-2. Save as corpus to `.omc/decision_corpus.json`\r","42030:3. On UserPromptSubmit: BM25-rank against query, inject top-7\r","42031-\r","42032-### Hybrid Architecture\r","--","42033-\r","42034:Combine proactive + reactive:\r","42035-1. **Proactive** (session start): inject last 3 decisions (recency signal)\r","42036-2. **Reactive** (query time): BM25 top-4 from full corpus\r","--","42037-\r","--","42100-The closed-set BM25 advantage (0.881) is largely an artifact of pre-filtered corpus construction. In realistic open-set:\r","42101-- **Dense embedding is the most reliable baseline** for temporal recall across arbitrary repos\r","42102:- **BM25 with pre-filtered corpus** (G1 decision commits) is still strong for CTX's specific use case (own project commits with consistent format)\r","42103-- **full_dump** fails on mature repos where relevant changes are older\r","42104-\r","--","42105-### Note on Sample Size and LLM Variance\r","42106-\r","42107:16 quality-filtered QA pairs (vs 9 initial). LLM scoring has ±0.1 variance across runs at this scale. Results are directionally stable but 50+ pairs per repo would be needed for production benchmarking.\r","42108-\r","42109----\r","--","42113-The full evaluation (59 QA pairs, 7 baselines, 413 LLM calls) reveals:\r","42114-\r","42115:1. **BM25 retrieval (0.881) is the dominant approach** — 23.7% higher recall than full dump, 5.2x higher than current CTX git_memory, at 17.5x smaller context\r","42116-2. **Current CTX git_memory_real (0.169) needs architectural upgrade** — query-agnostic proactive injection fundamentally cannot achieve high recall across temporal horizons\r","42117-3. **Dense embedding (0.644) is a viable alternative** — more robust across age buckets than BM25 but lower peak accuracy\r","--","42118-4. **0.000 recall for 7-30d commits in all proactive methods** — proactive injection is inherently limited to recent history\r","--","42119-5. **Recommended upgrade**: hybrid proactive (3 recency decisions) + BM25 reactive (4 query-relevant decisions)\r","42120-6. **[Open-Set Revision]** BM25 closed-set result (0.881) is optimistic — open-set eval (16 quality-filtered pairs, 3 repos) shows BM25=0.250 (−72%). Dense embedding is the most robust method in open-set (0.375), consistent with its age-robustness in closed-set evaluation. BM25 pre-filtered corpus is still the right choice for CTX's own repo context.\r","--","42121-7. **[2026-04-09 Hook Deployment]** bm25-memory.py deployed as production hook (replaces git-memory.py). G2-DOCS eval: 10/10 (100%) vs PageIndex 3/10 (30%). G1 git-decision corpus: 163 decisions indexed, multi-project verified (CTX/Entity/FromScratch). Bug fix: BM25 threshold >0→>3.0 eliminates false positives from incidental Korean token matches.\r","--","42122-8. **[2026-04-09 G1 Structural Verification]** bm25-memory.py hook verified against 59 QA pairs: corpus coverage 59/59 (100%), BM25 Structural Recall@7 = **1.000** (59/59). All ground-truth commits present in 163-commit corpus and ranked top-7 by BM25. End-to-end recall ≥0.881 (structural floor; LLM extraction is the only remaining variance). **G1 verification complete.**\r","42123-\r","--","42124-## Related\r","42125:- benchmark\r","--","42767-### 핵심 코드 변경\r","42768-\r","42769:**_bm25_rank_by_prompt()** — 신규 함수:\r","42770-```python\r","42771-def _bm25_rank_by_prompt(candidates, prompt_keywords):\r","--","42772-    from rank_bm25 import BM25Okapi\r","42773-    tokenized_corpus = [re.findall(r'[a-zA-Z가-힣][a-zA-Z0-9가-힣]*',\r","--","42774-                                    c[\"subject\"].lower()) for c in    r = subprocess.run([\"git\", \"log\", \"--format=%H\\x1f%s\",\r","42792-                        f\"--grep={kw}\", \"-i\", \"--max-count=5\"], ...)\r","42793-    # _is_decision() 필터 후 candidates에 One pip install.\"","46860-","--","46900-## Abstract (Key Points)\r","46901-\r","46902:- **Problem**: LLM long context windows are inefficient for code intelligence tasks.\r","46903-  \"Lost in the Middle\" (Shi et al., ACL 2024) shows U-shaped attention degradation.\r","46904-  LongCodeBench: Claude 3.5 accuracy drops from 29% (32K) to 3% (256K).\r","--","46905-  Even with perfect retrieval, context length alone causes 13.9-85% performance loss.\r","46906-\r","46907:- **Method**: 4-type trigger classifier + 3-tier hierarchical memory + import graph traversal.\r","46908-  Queries are classified into EXPLICIT_SYMBOL, SEMANTIC_CONCEPT, TEMPORAL_HISTORY, IMPLICIT_CONTEXT.\r","46909-  Each type activates a specialized retrieval strategy with adaptive-k selection.\r","--","46919-### Motivation: Human Associative Memory Model\r","46920-- Human brain does not keep all memories active simultaneously\r","46921:- Trigger input -> relevant memories activate -> load into working memory -> process -> return to idle\r","46922-- This optimizes both energy efficiency and processing speed\r","46923-\r","--","46924-### Problem Statement\r","--","46925-- Current LLM context management: dump everything into context window\r","46926-- Fundamental limitation: attention mechanism dilutes across long context\r","--","46963-\r","46964-### 3.2 Hierarchical Memory Architecture\r","46965:- **Working Memory (Tier 1)**: currently active context, recently accessed files\r","46966-- **Episodic Memory (Tier 2)**: session history, previously discussed modules\r","46967-- **Semantic Memory (Tier 3)**: long-term knowledge indices (symbol, concept, import graph)\r","--","46968-- Tier selection driven by trigger type\r","--","46969-\r","--","46970-### 3.3 Import Graph Traversal (Core Differentiator)\r","--","46984-### 4.1 Datasets\r","46985-- **Synthetic** (small: 50 files, 166 queries): Zipf-distributed file references, controlled import graph, 4 trigger types\r","46986:- **Real codebase** (GraphPrompt: 73 files, 80 queries): actual Python project with ast-extracted ground truth\r","46987-\r","46988-### 4.2 Baselines (5 strategies)\r","--","47011-| **Adaptive Trigger** | **0.880** | **0.052** | **0.780** |\r","47012-\r","47013:#### Main Results Table (Real: GraphPrompt)\r","47014-| Strategy | Recall@5 | Token Eff. | TES |\r","47015-|----------|----------|-----------|-----|\r","--","47040-### 5.3 Code Structure Utilization (vs Memori)\r","47041-- Memori: pure embedding-based, no structural awareness\r","47042:- CTX: import graph enables 150% improvement on dependency queries\r","47043-- Trade-off: CTX requires code parsing; Memori is language-agnostic\r","47044-\r","--","47052-\r","47053-### Conclusion\r","47054:- Trigger-driven dynamic context loading achieves 41.1x TES improvement over full context\r","47055-- Import graph traversal is essential for implicit dependency queries\r","47056-- Token efficiency (5% usage) enables practical deployment in cost-sensitive environments\r","--","47057-\r","47058-### Future Work (P2 and beyond)\r","--","47059-1. **Real LLM API [[projects/CTX/infra/20260423-wsl2-remote-tailscale-tunneling|20260423-wsl2-remote-tailscale-tunneling]]","70479-</file>","--","70480-","--","70484-\r","70485-## Original Question\r","70486:If a client PC's browser accesses `http://localhost:3003` (mirrored via netsh portproxy + Tailscale to WSL2 dev server), does login state sync to a browser on the WSL2 side?\r","70487-\r","70488-## Web Facts\r","--","70489:- [FACT-1] Cookies stored per-browser-profile in the user's cookie jar, not in URL origin state. Same URL does NOT imply shared cookies across browsers/machines. (w3tutorials.net)\r","70490-- [FACT-2] Localhost cookies ARE shared across ports on same browser (RFC 6265 port-agnostic), BUT `localhost` and `127.0.0.1` are treated as different domains — cookies don't cross. (node-security.com)\r","70491-- [FACT-3] Playwright `storageState()` exports cookies + localStorage + sessionStorage + IndexedDB to JSON. `.storageState({path: 'auth.json'})` saves; `browser.newContext({storageState: 'auth.json'})` loads. Standard pattern for test automation. (playwright.dev/docs/auth)\r","--","70500-### Detailed\r","70501-\r","70502:(a) **Cookies sync across profiles?** NO. Each browser (client Chrome, WSL2's browser or Playwright) has its own cookie jar. Same-origin URL doesn't help.\r","70503-\r","70504-(b) **Legitimate way to port login state?** YES — Playwright's `storageState`:\r","--","70519-\r","70520-(d) **Security concerns with same-URL port-forward:**\r","70521:- Any process on the client PC can access `localhost:3003` and inherit the authenticated browser's cookies (if cookies are SameSite=Lax and the app doesn't check origin strictly)\r","70522-- `storageState.json` files are equivalent to stolen credentials — never commit to git, never share outside trusted context\r","70523-- If Tailscale node is compromised, attacker gets the tunnel endpoint on the attacker's machine\r","--","70524-- Mitigations: use SameSite=Strict on auth cookies in the dev app; never commit storageState; use short-lived tokens in dev\r","70525-\r","--","70541-\r","70542-## Related\r","70543:- [[projects/CTX/research/20260424-claude-client-bootstrap-session-summary|20260424-claude-client-bootstrap-session-summary]]\r","70544-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260424-common-port-fanout-tailscale-vs-ssh|20260424-common-port-fanout-tailscale-vs-ssh]]\r","70545-- [[projects/CTX/infra/20260423-wsl2-remote-tailscale-tunneling|20260423-wsl2-remote-tailscale-tunneling]]\r","--","70546-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260325-long-session-context-management|20260325-long-session-context-management]]\r","--","70547-- [[projects/CTX/decisions/20260326-path-derived-module-to-file|20260326-path-derived-module-to-file]]","--","70548-</file>","--","70549-","--","70584-```\r","70585-\r","70586:That's it. \r","--","78701-**Scope**: MCP servers in-project + production tools (Cursor, Aider, Continue.dev, LanceDB, tree-sitter, Codebase-Memory)  \r","--","78702-**Goal**: Find practical, non-academic tools (R@5=0.595)**. \r","78709:Five production tools exist with complementary strengths:\r","78710-\r","78711-1. **Cursor** — Two-stage re-ranking (embedding + LLM re-rank)\r","--","78712-2. **Aider** — PageRank on dependency graph + token optimization\r","78713:3. **Continue.dev** — LanceDB vector DB + context providers (pluggable)\r","78714-4. **Tree-sitter** — AST parsing for 66+ languages (accuracy boost)\r","78715-5. **Codebase-Memory MCP** — Already in-project! Tree-sitter knowledge graph, 14 tools\r","--","78716-\r","78717-**Recommendation**: Integrate Codebase-Memory MCP + tree-sitter for external codebase indexing; \r","--","78718:adopt LanceDB for vector fallback (vs current embedding-free approach); \r","78719-implement Aider's PageRank + token budget optimization.\r","78720-\r","--","78721----\r","78722-\r","78723:## 1. MCP Servers Already Available (In-Project)\r","78724-\r","78725-### A. mcp__codebase-memory-mcp__ (Codebase-Memory)\r","--","78726:**Status**: Already installed in CTX project  \r","78727-**GitHub**: [DeusData/codebase-memory-mcp](https://github.com/DeusData/codebase-memory-mcp)\r","78728-\r","--","78748-**Can CTX use it?**  \r","78749-**YES — Already available!** CTX currently underutilizes it.\r","78750:- **Improvement potential**: External codebase R@5 from 0.152 → 0.40+ (estimated)\r","78751-  - Current CTX on Flask/Requests/FastAPI: R@5 = 0.15 (heuristic + BM25)\r","78752-  - Codebase-Memory would leverage AST for symbol accuracy\r","--","78753-- **How**: Route implicit/semantic queries to `trace_call_path()` + `search_graph()` \r","78754-  instead of falling back to regex-based symbol extraction\r","78755:- **Cost**: ~10-30ms additional latency per query (vs BM25 <1ms)\r","78756-\r","78757-**Risk**: Over-reliance on structural queries for semantic concepts.\r","--","78787-- **Current gap**: CTX's semantic routing (0.0231 R@5 on external codebases)\r","78788-- **Solution**: Use mcp__code-search__ as SEMANTIC_CONCEPT fallback when CTX scores low\r","78789:- **Cost**: 20-30s cold-start indexing per repo\r","78790-- **Improvement**: External SEMANTIC queries R@5 from 0.0231 → 0.15-0.25 (estimated)\r","78791-\r","--","78792-**Existing Eval in CTX**:\r","--","78809-**YES** — Cache search results between sessions\r","78810-- Store BM25 query results + rankings between sessions\r","78811:- Recall common query patterns (e.g., \"auth flow\" across projects)\r","78812-- **Improvement**: Reduce index rebuild overhead for identical/similar queries\r","78813-- **Cost**: Minimal (entity store, <1ms)\r","--","78814-\r","--","78815----\r","--","78816-\r","78817:## 2. [[projects/CTX/research/20260327-ctx-real-project-self-eval|20260327-ctx-real-project-self-eval]]","--","79176-</file>","--","79177-","--","79178:<file path=\"docs/research/20260402-project-understanding-evaluation-framework.md\">","79179-# Project Understanding Evaluation Framework\r","79180-**Research Date**: 2026-04-02  \r","--","79181-**Researcher**: Explorer  \r","--","79182:**Goal**: Comprehensive evaluation methods for measuring \"project understanding\" in AI systems\r","79183-\r","79184----\r","--","79186-## Executive Summary\r","79187-\r","79188:### Three Evaluation Approaches for G1 (Project Understanding)\r","79189-\r","79190-**G1 = Can an AI system understand a project's work history and direction using ONLY code/docs (no stored memory files, no git log access)?**\r","--","79191-\r","79192-After surveying existing academic benchmarks and CTX's current evaluation suite, I recommend a **three-tier framework**:\r","--","79193-\r","79194-1. **Retrieval-based (R@K, NDCG, MRR)** — Fast, standardized, correlates with downstream quality\r","--","79195:2. **Generation-based (hallucination rate, answer accuracy)** — Measures actual utility; reveals over-anchoring problems\r","79196-3. **Task-based (code completion pass@1, instruction grounding F1)** — True end-to-end metric; expensive but most realistic\r","79197-\r","--","79207-|------|---------|-------------|-----------------|\r","79208-| **downstream_llm_eval.py** | G1/G2 downstream quality | Generation-based (AA, FRA, HR) | G1: Δ+0.781 (1.000 vs 0.219) |\r","79209:| **project_understanding_g1_eval.py** | G1 project state understanding | Generation-based (keyword match) | Extracts ground truth from CLAUDE.md/docs |\r","79210-| **zero_storage_g1_eval.py** | G1 retrieval without persistent memory | Retrieval-based (Recall@K) | Tests EXPLICIT_SYMBOL, SEMANTIC_CONCEPT, IMPLICIT_CONTEXT |\r","79211-| **cross_session_recall.py** | G1 cross-session continuity | Retrieval-based (Recall@K) | Recall@10=0.567 (currently published goal) |\r","--","79219-Recall@K          \"Is relevant file in top-K?\"           Binary (0/1)\r","79220-NDCG@K            \"How highly ranked is relevant file?\"  0.0 - 1.0 (discounted by position)\r","79221:MRR               \"What's the reciprocal rank?\"          1/position\r","79222-MAP               \"Average precision across K?\"          Not currently used\r","79223-```\r","--","79293-- **Task**: Code localization (given a bug/feature request, find the right file(s))\r","79294-- **Result**: 92.7% file accuracy on SWE-Bench-Lite\r","79295:- **Graph-based approach**: Parses code into heterogeneous graph (files, classes, functions, imports)\r","79296-\r","79297-**LocAgent relevance to CTX**: Both solve \"file discovery\" but LocAgent includes:\r","--","79302-### 2.3 MemoryArena (Feb 2026) — NEW Benchmark for Long-Context Understanding\r","79303-\r","79304:**First benchmark specifically for \"project understanding across sessions\"**\r","79305-- Measures: Can AI recall previous work history after long gap?\r","79306-- Metrics: Recall@K of session history, memory persistence\r","--","79319-Interpretation: \"What % of relevant files did we find in top K?\"\r","79320-```\r","79321:- **Pros**: Simple, interpretable, fast (<1ms)\r","79322-- **Cons**: Doesn't penalize ranking order (file at position 5 == file at position 1)\r","79323-- **Use for G1**: YES — quick quality check\r","--","79330-Interpretation: \"How well are relevant files ranked?\"\r","79331-```\r","79332:- **Pros**: Accounts for ranking order, discounts lower positions\r","79333-- **Cons**: More complex; requires relevance scores (binary vs graded)\r","79334-- **Use for G1**: **STRONGLY YES** — correlates better with downstream LLM quality\r","--","79335-- **CTX result**: NDCG@5=0.830, NDCG@10=0.723\r","79336-\r","79337:#### MRR (Mean Reciprocal Rank)\r","79338-```\r","79339-MRR = 1/rank_of_first_relevant_item\r","--","79341-Interpretation: \"How quickly do we find the first relevant file?\"\r","79342-```\r","79343:- **Pros**: Single-number summary, captures \"first good result\"\r","79344-- **Cons**: Ignores all other relevant results beyond first\r","79345-- **Use for G1**: MAYBE — useful if you want top-1 quality (but R@1 is simpler)\r","--","79352-Range: 0.0 - 1.0\r","79353-```\r","79354:- **Pros**: Combines Recall@K and ranking quality\r","79355-- **Cons**: Less commonly used in modern literature (NDCG preferred)\r","79356-- **Use for G1**: NOT CURRENTLY — but could be useful for publication\r","--","79364-Interpretation: \"Did the LLM mention the right file/function names?\"\r","79365-```\r","79366:- **Pros**: Direct measurement of \"useful output\"\r","79367-- **Cons**: Requires careful keyword definition; sensitive to paraphrasing\r","79368-- **Use for G1**: **YES** — essential for goal 1 (memory recall)\r","--","79375-Interpretation: \"How much did the LLM make stuff up?\"\r","79376-```\r","79377:- **Pros**: Directly measures reliability\r","79378-- **Cons**: Requires ground truth of all valid filenames\r","79379-- **Use for G1**: **CRITICAL** — distinguishes \"helpful context\" from \"misleading context\"\r","--","79386-Range: 0.0 - 1.0\r","79387-```\r","79388:- **Pros**: Catches subtle quality degradation\r","79389-- **Cons**: Task-dependent (hard vs easy tasks behave differently)\r","79390-- **Use for G1**: **YES** — key finding from downstream eval\r","--","79391:- **CTX result**: 20% of tasks (context selection needs improvement)\r","79392-\r","79393-### 3.3 Task-Based Metrics (Gold Standard but Expensive)\r","--","79395-#### Pass@K\r","79396-```\r","79397:pass@K = % of K attempts that produce correct code\r","79398-Range: 0.0 - 1.0\r","79399-Uses: Multiple independent samples from LLM with temperature>0\r","--","79400-```\r","79401:- **Pros**: Measures actual code correctness (execution tested)\r","79402-- **Cons**: Expensive (K samples × code execution per task), slow\r","79403-- **Use for G1**: **IDEAL** — but only for subset of important tasks\r","--","79410-Recall    = |predicted_relevant_files ∩ actual_relevant| / |actual|\r","79411-```\r","79412:- **Pros**: Balanced metric for \"can you find all relevant files given an instruction?\"\r","79413-- **Cons**: Requires manual labeling of relevant files per instruction\r","79414-- **Use for G1**: **YES** — measures instruction→file grounding (your G2 goal)\r","--","79420-Range: 0.0 - 1.0\r","79421-```\r","79422:- **Pros**: Hardest metric; accounts for import errors, undefined functions\r","79423-- **Cons**: Requires sandboxed execution environment\r","79424-- **Use for G1**: FUTURE — advanced evaluation\r","--","79428-## Part 4: Recommended G1 Evaluation Framework\r","79429-\r","79430:### 4.1 Three-Tier Framework\r","79431-\r","79432-```\r","--","79433-┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\r","79434:│ TIER 1: Retrieval Quality (Fast, <1ms per query)                │\r","79435-├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\r","79436-│ Metrics: R@3, R@5, R@10, NDCG@5, NDCG@10, MRR                  │\r","--","79441-         ↓\r","79442-┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\r","79443:│ TIER 2: Generation Quality (Slow, ~5-10s per query with LLM)    │\r","79444-├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\r","79445-│ Metrics: Answer Accuracy, Hallucination Rate, Over-anchoring     │\r","--","79446:│ Protocol: WITH context vs WITHOUT context (A/B test)             │\r","79447-│ Purpose: Measure downstream utility + identify anchoring issues  │\r","79448-│ Tool: downstream_llm_eval.py + project_understanding_g1_eval.py │\r","--","79449-│ Sample size: 10-20 scenarios (cost: $5-20 per run)              │\r","79450-└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\r","--","79451-         ↓\r","--","79452-┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\r","79453:│ TIER 3: Task Completion (Very Slow, ~30s per task)              │\r","79454-├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\r","79455-│ Metrics: Pass@1 (code generation), Task completion accuracy      │\r","--","79456:│ Protocol: Real developer tasks (bug fix, feature add)            │\r","79457-│ Purpose: True end-to-end quality measurement                     │\r","79458-│ Tool: (NEW) real_task_completion_eval.py (proposed)              │\r","--","79459-│ Sample size: 5-10 real tasks (cost: $20-50 per run)             │\r","79460-└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\r","--","79461-```\r","--","79480-Method              R@5      NDCG@5   AA       HR       Notes\r","79481-─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\r","79482:CTX (adaptive)      0.954    0.830    0.95     0.02     Proposed\r","79483-BM25 (TF-IDF)       0.833    0.655    0.72     0.08     Standard IR baseline\r","79484-LlamaIndex Dense    0.900    0.610    0.78     0.10     Dense vector baseline\r","--","79541-def measure_arm():\r","79542-    # Manual: Ask LLM \"Draw the architecture\" with context\r","79543:    llm_output = llm_with_context(\"Describe the project architecture\")\r","79544-    \r","79545-    # Score: Does it mention N main components?\r","--","79604----\r","79605-\r","79606:## Part 6: Comparison: CTX vs Existing Approaches\r","79607-\r","79608-### 6.1 CTX vs CodeSearchNet Metrics\r","--","79612-| **Task** | Code→NL search | NL→code search (opposite) |\r","79613-| **Metrics** | NDCG, MRR, MAP | R@K, NDCG (+ AA, HR) |\r","79614:| **Code Coverage** | Single functions | Entire projects |\r","79615-| **Dependencies** | Ignored | Modeled (import BFS) |\r","79616-| **Publication Use** | Standard | Not yet standard |\r","--","79623-|--------|------|-----|\r","79624-| **Task Coverage** | 8 tasks across 7 domains | 4 trigger types + docs |\r","79625:| **Datasets** | 10 (CodeSearchNet, CosQA, etc.) | Custom (CTX project, external) |\r","79626-| **Metrics** | R@K, NDCG, MAP, EM | R@K, NDCG, MRR + AA, HR, DRR Generalization strategy must preserve this moat\r","79898:- Framing: \"how to expand CTX's unique capability to other project styles\" (not \"how to match Cursor\")\r","79899-\r","79900-**MAJOR — Hybrid Breaks <1ms Latency Promise**\r","--","79901-- CTX's current value prop: deterministic, <1ms, no LLM calls\r","79902-- Dense embedding: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 = ~50ms on CPU (not <1ms)\r","--","79903-- Must address: async pre-computation, two-stage (BM25 filter → dense rerank top-5), or explicit latency disclosure\r","--","79904-\r","79905:**MAJOR — No Ablation Plan**\r","79906-- Which factor (keywords / IDF / corpus composition) causes 0.250?\r","79907-- Single experiment: set `_is_decision()` = identity function (all commits), re-measure open-set → isolates keyword effect\r","--","79918-| FACT-2 decision verbs for `_is_decision()` | CONFIRMED (empirical) | VALID addition | CONFIRMED-STRONG |\r","79919-| CTX cross-session uniqueness | CONFIRMED (FACT-5/6/7/8 all absent) | CRITICAL gap | CONFIRMED-STRONG |\r","79920:| Regex/CC pattern tier | CONTRADICTED (F1=0.76 ceiling) | MAJOR critique | CONTESTED — qualify heavily |\r","79921-| G2a dynamic path discovery | CONFIRMED (no contradiction) | MINOR fix | CONFIRMED |\r","79922-| Korean tokenizer benign | CONFIRMED | Mostly agreed | STRONG |\r","--","79923-| Hybrid breaks latency | New (DA) | MAJOR | CONTESTED — tradeoff |\r","79924:| Lore Protocol opt-in | FACT-3 (no benchmark) | UNRESOLVED | UNRESOLVED |\r","79925-\r","79926----\r","--","79947-_RATIONALE_SIGNALS = 3)\r","--","79953-```\r","--","79954-\r","--","79962-\r","79963-# 개선: 프로젝트 루트에서 glob 스캔\r","79964:def discover_doc_paths(project_dir: Path) -> list[str]:\r","79965-    candidates = [\"docs/**/*.md\", 수치(FACT-11: +15-30%)는 commit message 도메인에서 검증되지 않음** — 기술 문서 기준값. commit messages는 더 짧고 noisier → 최적 비율이 다를 수 있음.\r","80004:- **Open-set 0.250 eval protocol**: CTX closed-set(손수 QA 59쌍)과 bound).\r","80006-\r","--","80009-1. **Ablation 실험** (1시간): `_is_decision()` = identity → open-set recall 측정 → 진짜 원인 규명\r","80010-2. **Commit message dense embedding** recall 측정: all-MiniLM-L6-v2로 Flask/Django/Requests eval\r","80011:3. **5+ project style** open-set eval harness 구축 (현재 N=3 — 통계적으로 부족)\r","80012-4. **hybrid retrieval latency** 실측: two-stage BM25→dense rerank 실제 지연 [REASONING] 현재 CTX는 강제성(deterministic hook)을 강점으로 삼지만, 스킬 기반 접근은 사용자가 필요할 때만 호출하는 advisory 방식이다. 두 방식의 hybrid: hook이 기본 context를 주입하고, `/spark` 스킬이 패턴 발견을 on-demand로 실행하는 조합이 가능하다.\r","--","80308-CTX의 가장 강력한 차별점은 LLM 호출 없이 <1ms로 작동하는 deterministic context bootstrapping이다. claude-vault는 Obsidian Markdown 변환과 Ollama LLM 태깅을 핵심으로 한다.\r","80309-\r","80310:이 두 시스템의 통합은 CTX의 속도 보장을 파기할 위험이 있다. claude-vault sync를 CTX hook 체인에 포함시키면 UserPromptSubmit hook의 latency가 LLM 태깅 시간만큼 증가한다. llama3.2:3b의 추론 시간은 수백 ms ~ 수 초다. [REASONING] 이는 CTX의 \"deterministic, <1ms\" 가치 제안과 근본적으로 양립할 수 없다.\r","80311-\r","80312-**결론**: claude-vault는 CTX hook 체인 안에 넣으면 안 된다. 비동기 후처리 레이어로만 사용해야 한다.\r","--","80332-**적용 2 (단기, 비동기 레이어): .jsonl → G1 보강 파이프라인**\r","80333-\r","80334:`claude-vault sync ~/.claude`를 CTX hook 외부(cron job 또는 세션 종료 후 별도 실행)에서 실행해 .jsonl을 Markdown으로 변환한다. 변환된 Markdown을 BM25 인덱스에 추가한다. 이 방식은 <1ms hook latency를 유지하면서 G1 데이터 소스를 확장한다. 핵심 조건: UserPromptSubmit hook 체인에 포함하지 않는다.\r","80335-\r","80336-구체적 파일: `~/.claude/hooks/git-memory.py`의 BM25 인덱스 구축 부분에 `~/.claude-vault/exported/` 디렉토리를 추가 소스로 포함.\r","--","80434-### 절대 금지\r","80435-\r","80436:**Ollama 태깅을 UserPromptSubmit hook에 포함하지 말 것** — llama3.2:3b 추론이 수백ms이므로 CTX의 `<1ms deterministic` 핵심 가치 파기.\r","80437-\r","80438----\r","--","80463-> **G1** (time memory): \"20260405 Old CTX remnants fully removed\" and 6 more\r","80464-> **G2** (space search): `downstream_llm_eval.py`, `hybrid_dense_ctx.py` — found via \"CTX 별도 전역\"\r","80465:> [GraphPrompt] task: coding | condition: F\r","80466-\r","80467-즉, CTX hook과 별개로 **`~/.claude/` 전역 레벨**에서 claude-vault를 쓰겠다는 것이군요.\r","--","80536-~/.obsidian/Claude/           ← claude-vault output\r","80537-  conversations/\r","80538:    2026-04-10-ctx-project.md\r","80539-    2026-04-10-imtoon-problem.md\r","80540-  by-project/\r","--","80541-  by-date/\r","--","80542-```\r","--","80571-> **G1** (time memory): \"20260403 command hook + additionalContext for SOTA G1 activation\" and 6 more\r","80572-> **G2** (space search): `CLAUDE.md`, `MCP_AUTOMATION_RESEARCH.md` — found via \"자동 아카이빙 claude hook\"\r","80573:> [GraphPrompt] task: reasoning | condition: E\r","80574-\r","80575-**다른 claude-vault 가 있군요.** `cargo install` = Rust 기반으로 별개 프로젝트입니다. 바로 조사합니다.\r","--","80654-\r","80655-```bash\r","80656:claude-vault search \"BM25 threshold\" --project CTX\r","80657-claude-vault search \"query\" --json | claude  # [[projects/CTX/research/20260411-hook-comparison-auto-index-vs-chat-memory|20260411-hook-comparison-auto-index-vs-chat-memory]]","--","82554-</file>","--","82555-","--","82556-<file path=\"docs/research/20260417-ctx-semantic-search-upgrade-sota.md\">","82557:# [expert-research-v2] CTX Semantic Search Upgrade to SOTA Tier\r","82558-**Date**: 2026-04-17  **Skill**: expert-research-v2\r","82559-\r","--","82560-## Original Question\r","82561:How can we upgrade CTX's semantic search ability to SOTA tier while preserving <1ms deterministic hook latency?\r","82562-\r","82563-## Current State Snapshot\r","--","82573-[FACT-2] SPLADE at query time adds 100-300ms. Mitigation: pre-compute document SPLADE vectors; run SPLADE only on query. (blog.premai.io)\r","82574-\r","82575:[FACT-3] ColBERT produces per-token vector matrices for late interaction reranking. (infiniflow.org)\r","82576-\r","82577-[FACT-4] \"Blended RAG\" (full-text + dense + sparse) + ColBERT reranker is optimal at scale. (infiniflow.org)\r","--","82590-(modal.com, jina.ai, voyageai.com)\r","82591-\r","82592:[FACT-9] HyDE pitfalls: without grounded corpus knowledge only marginal improvement + hallucination risk. (arxiv.org/2509.07794)\r","82593-\r","82594-[FACT-10] LLM query expansion causes query drift, degrades retrieval accuracy. (arxiv.org, mdpi.com)\r","--","82602-### Domain Expert (Lens 1) — 6 Insights\r","82603-\r","82604:1. **SPLADE query-only highest-ROI semantic upgrade** [GROUNDED via FACT-2] — Pre-compute doc vectors at index time, daemon handles query encoding. 30-80ms daemon cost acceptable with async timeout pattern.\r","82605-2. **G2 SQL LIKE replacement is largest addressable gap** [REASONED + GROUNDED via FACT-5] — Zero IDF, zero TF. Binary match/no-match. Replace with in-process BM25 (rank_bm25 already installed).\r","82606-3. **Code embedding ROI depends on query type, not benchmark averages** [UNCERTAIN] — voyage-code-3's +16.3% was measured at large scale. CTX's 30-200 doc corpora are IDF-advantaged regime (FACT-5).\r","--","82607-4. **_SYNONYM_MAP → corpus co-occurrence mining** [REASONED] — Avoids FACT-10 LLM drift; static pre-computation. Trade-off: n=170 commits has high co-occurrence variance.\r","--","82612-\r","82613-1. **[OVERCONFIDENT + CONFLICT]** SPLADE/dense recommendations conflict with FACT-5 and FACT-6. Sourcegraph has vastly more resources and deprecated embeddings. SPLADE was trained to fix BM25's weakness on large corpora — CTX doesn't have that weakness.\r","82614:2. **[MISSING]** <1ms is about hook process, not daemon. SPLADE's 30-80ms CPU encoding means frequent timeout fallback. Semantic contributes \"sometimes\" — rarely enough to shift recall metrics.\r","82615-3. **[MISSING]** G2 LIKE→BM25 has highest verified impact, zero latency cost, zero new deps, already-installed lib. Tier 1 priority inverted in Lens 1.\r","82616-4. **[CONFLICT]** \"Semantic upgrade is right priority\" challenged by eval: G1 ceiling ~0.80, FastAPI \"50% structurally ambiguous\" per MEMORY.md. Any semantic gain may fall within noise band, untestable.\r","--","82617-\r","--","82618:### Synthesis (Lens 3) — 3-Tier Roadmap\r","82619-\r","82620-**Tier 1 (1-day wins, high confidence):**\r","--","82621-- **T1-A: G2 SQL LIKE → in-process BM25** — `git-memory.py` G2 section + `auto-index.py`. <0.5ms, expected +0.10-0.20 FastAPI R@5.\r","82622-- **T1-B: Corpus-derived co-occurrence synonyms** — `auto-index.py` scans git log, top-20 co-occurring pairs (>30%) written to `synonym_extensions.json`. +2-5pp G1 recall on 0-7d bucket.\r","--","82623-- **T1-C: Query-type-aware RRF weights** — `git-memory.py` fusion. Detect keyword vs NL, weight accordingly. +2-4pp on keyword queries.\r","--","82624-\r","82625:**Tier 2 (1-week, moderate confidence):**\r","82626-- **T2-A: SPLADE doc pre-computation + query-only** — `naver/splade-cocondenser-ensemble-distil`, 110M, ~40-80ms daemon query. [UNCERTAIN] domain transfer from MS-MARCO to git commits. Ablate first.\r","82627-- **T2-B: Persistent BM25 index in daemon** — eliminate rebuild latency. G1 hot path 5-15ms → <1ms.\r","--","82628-\r","82629:**Tier 3 (2-4 weeks, SOTA tier, conditional):**\r","82630-- **T3-A: voyage-code-3 or jina-v2-base-code for G2 NL→Code ONLY** — gated by query classifier. ~50-200ms daemon. [CONFLICT with FACT-6] Cody deprecated this; use narrowly.\r","82631-- **T3-B: ColBERT-lite late interaction rerank top-20** — ~20-50ms. ~12MB storage for 200-doc corpus. [UNCERTAIN] small-corpus benefit.\r","--","82642-## Final Conclusion\r","82643-\r","82644:**Do Tier 1 first. Period.** The highest-impact, lowest-risk upgrade is replacing G2's SQL `LIKE %keyword%` with in-process BM25 — this uses already-installed tooling, adds zero latency, and directly addresses the FastAPI R@5=0.40 failure through FACT-5's IDF mechanism. Corpus-derived synonyms (T1-B) and query-type-aware RRF (T1-C) are zero-cost additions.\r","82645-\r","82646-**Tier 2/3 require ablation before rollout.** Both FACT-5 (BM25 advantage on small corpora) and FACT-6 (Cody's embedding deprecation) are warnings that CTX's known Flask -0.106 regression is structural, not a model-capacity problem. Upgrading from multilingual-e5-small to voyage-code-3 may not reverse it — the IDF advantage on small corpora is a statistical phenomenon independent of model quality.\r","--","82647-\r","82648-**The Cody architecture (FACT-7) validates CTX's hot/cold split.** Cody uses local-only for latency-critical paths, remote for chat. CTX's <1ms hook constraint places G1/G2/CM squarely in the \"local-only\" regime. The vec-daemon should provide asynchronous enrichment, never block the hot path.\r","--","82649-\r","82650-**Confidence: MEDIUM-HIGH.** Tier 1 is high confidence. Tier 2/3 has uncertain small-corpus transfer; require ablation before commit.\r","--","82651-\r","82652-## Sources\r","--","82653:- [Hybrid Search in RAG: Dense + Sparse (BM25/SPLADE)](https://blog.gopenai.com/hybrid-search-in-rag-dense-sparse-bm25-splade-reciprocal-rank-fusion-and-when-to-use-which-fafe4fd6156e)\r","82654-- [Hybrid Search for RAG: BM25, SPLADE, and Vector Search Combined](https://blog.premai.io/hybrid-search-for-rag-bm25-splade-and-vector-search-combined/)\r","82655-- [Sparse vs Dense Retrieval for RAG](https://mljourney.com/sparse-vs-dense-retrieval-for-rag-bm25-embeddings-and-hybrid-search/)\r","--","82675-**T1-A (G2 SQL LIKE → BM25) was implemented, failed an A/B on the critical test\r","82676-case, and REVERTED. Initial root-cause diagnosis was overreaching and is now\r","82677:marked as unproven — see Honest Correction below.**\r","82678-\r","82679-### A/B Harness (iteration 2 — with fixed code-identifier tokenizer)\r","--","82685-|-------|-----------|-----------|---------|\r","82686-| \"BM25 retriever lookup\" | `rank_bm25`, `bm25_score`, `bm25_retrieve`, `bm25_retriever.py` | `DenseRetriever`, `LlamaIndexRetriever`, `ChromaDenseRetriever`, `chroma_retrieve…` | **0/5** |\r","82687:| \"search graph prompt\" | `load_codesearchnet_corpus`, `mcp_code_search_headtohead.md` | `mcp_code_search_headtohead.md`, `benchmarks/results/…` | 3/5 |\r","82688-| \"semantic rerank\" | `_detect_semantic_concepts`, `test_related_to_snake_case_stays_semantic` | `_detect_semantic_concepts`, `test_function_keyword_no_underscore…` | 3/5 |\r","82689-| \"fair eval\" | `evaluate`, `run_eval`, `eval_repo` | `EvalResult`, `eval_surfacing`, `RealEvalResult` | 0/5 |\r","--","82708-   harness bug, not a scoring-theory issue.\r","82709-2. Second run (fixed tokenizer): T1-A still fails the canonical case, but\r","82710:   the mechanism is **not yet proven to be IDF flattening**. It could also\r","82711-   be multi-token query scoring spreading weight across candidates that\r","82712-   match only one token, length-normalization penalising short names\r","--","82717-The accurate statement is:\r","82718-- **T1-A as implemented regresses on the canonical symbol-lookup query.**\r","82719:- **The mechanism is not proven.** It is plausibly related to BM25's token-\r","82720-  level scoring behaviour on multi-token queries against short symbol names,\r","82721-  but this has not been measured.\r","--","82732-| T1-B (co-occurrence synonyms) | ⏸ pending — own A/B required before commit |\r","82733-| T1-C (query-type RRF alpha) | ⏸ pending — own A/B required; affects G1 decisions, not G2 symbols |\r","82734:| Tier 2 (SPLADE docs / daemon persistent BM25) | Remains low priority; no new evidence for or against |\r","82735-| Tier 3 (voyage-code-3 / ColBERT) | Remains deprioritized; not confirmed by this test |\r","82736-\r","--","82737-### What this run actually showed (bounded claim)\r","--","82738:- A naive BM25 replacement of `search_graph_for_prompt` as written in this session\r","82739-  loses to the existing LIKE + length-ASC ordering on queries where a specific\r","82740-  token like `bm25` uniquely identifies the target. This is reproducible on the\r","--","82741-  current CTX `codebase-memory-mcp` DB.\r","82742-- It did NOT show that BM25 is fundamentally wrong for symbol corpora, nor that\r","--","82746-- Validate each surface-level change with its own A/B before committing to a\r","82747-  general theory.\r","82748:- A harness bug (tokenizer) can produce results that look like a principled\r","82749-  failure mode. Always print a few per-candidate scores and tokens before\r","82750-  generalising from the ranking output.\r","--","82751-\r","82752-## Related\r","82753:- [[projects/CTX/research/20260426-ctx-retrieval-benchmark-synthesis|20260426-ctx-retrieval-benchmark-synthesis]]\r","82754-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260426-g1-hybrid-rrf-dense-retrieval|20260426-g1-hybrid-rrf-dense-retrieval]]\r","82755-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260426-g2-docs-eval-corpus-drift-fix|20260426-g2-docs-eval-corpus-drift-fix]]\r","--","82756-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260426-g2-docs-hybrid-dense-retrieval|20260426-g2-docs-hybrid-dense-retrieval]]\r","--","82757-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260407-g1-final-eval-benchmark|20260407-g1-final-eval-benchmark]]\r","--","82758-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260411-hook-comparison-auto-index-vs-chat-memory|20260411-hook-comparison-auto-index-vs-chat-memory]]\r","--","82759-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260408-g1-longterm-eval-initial-results|20260408-g1-longterm-eval-initial-results]]\r","--","82760-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260424-memory-retrieval-benchmark-landscape|20260424-memory-retrieval-benchmark-landscape]]","--","82761-</file>","--","82762-","--","82771-## Web Facts\r","82772-\r","82773:[FACT-1] Custom slash commands: markdown files in `~/.claude/commands/[name].md` (user-level) or `.claude/commands/[name].md` (project-level). Filename without `.md` = command name. `$ARGUMENTS` captures args passed after the command. (source: https://skillsplayground.com/guides/claude-code-slash-commands/, https://en.bioerrorlog.work/entry/claude-code-custom-slash-command)\r","82774-\r","82775-[FACT-2] Skills replaced commands as the recommended path in Claude Code v2.1.101 (2026-04-11), unified under `.claude/skills/` but old `.claude/commands/` still works. Skills support YAML frontmatter for `allowed-tools`, `model`, `description`. (source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/skills, https://supalaunch.com/blog/claude-code-skills-tutorial-custom-slash-commands-and-automations-guide)\r","--","82776-\r","82777:[FACT-3] Slash command frontmatter can restrict `allowed-tools` (e.g., only Bash), set model, and provide description shown in `/help`. (source: https://skillsplayground.com/guides/claude-code-slash-commands/)\r","82778-\r","82779-[FACT-4] Status line: customizable bar at bottom of Claude Code. Configured in `~/.claude/settings.json` under `statusLine: { type: \"command\", command: \"sh ~/my-script.sh\" }`. Runs any shell script, receives JSON session data (model, context_window.used_percentage, cost, workspace, rate_limits) on stdin. Runs on EACH update, not one-shot. Supports ANSI colors + OSC 8 clickable links. (source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/statusline)\r","--","82780-\r","82781-[FACT-5] MCP vs slash command: \"Use an MCP server if you need Claude to access an external tool, API, or data source; use a skill if you need to change how Claude writes code/communicates/thinks; use a hook if you need something to happen automatically when Claude acts; use a command if you need a reusable prompt shortcut.\" (source: https://www.morphllm.com/claude-code-skills-mcp-plugins, https://skiln.co/blog/claude-code-plugins-vs-skills-vs-mcp-decision-guide)\r","--","82782-\r","82783-[FACT-6] MCP resources are `@`-mentionable, appear in autocomplete alongside files. MCP servers connect via stdio (local process) or SSE. For a local script that's a report generator (not repeatedly queried during Claude's reasoning), MCP is overkill. (source: https://code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp)\r","--","82784-\r","82785-## Multi-Lens Analysis\r","--","82787-### Domain Expert (Lens 1) — integration patterns\r","82788-\r","82789:1. **Slash command is the idiomatic \"run script, inline output in chat\" pattern** [GROUNDED: FACT-1, FACT-5]. `~/.claude/commands/ctx-status.md` with `allowed-tools: [Bash]` and prompt `Run python3 ~/.claude/hooks/ctx-report.py $ARGUMENTS and output stdout verbatim`. Smallest possible change; zero new dependencies.\r","82790-\r","82791-2. **Status line is a POOR fit for on-demand reports** [GROUNDED: FACT-4]. Runs on every Claude Code update → either constant noise OR a 1-line squeeze that loses the report's real value. Ambient indicators (event count, daemon health) WOULD fit, but that violates \"stay out of it\" discipline.\r","--","82799-1. **[OVERCONFIDENT]** \"Slash command idiomatic\" — the newer pattern is SKILLS (v2.1.101). Commands still work but are legacy. For this trivial pass-through, skill frontmatter adds complexity. Resolution: use `.claude/commands/` deliberately — simpler is right here.\r","82800-\r","82801:2. **[MISSING]** Claude may reformat the script's output — model default is to summarize. Fix: prompt must explicitly say \"output stdout VERBATIM, do not summarize, do not rephrase.\"\r","82802-\r","82803-3. **[MISSING]** Discoverability — slash commands only appear in `/help` if `description:` is set in frontmatter.\r","--","82817-  - `alias ctxr='python3 ~/.claude/hooks/ctx-report.py'`\r","82818-\r","82819:Both together cost ~12 minutes, zero ambient noise, works in both surfaces (in-chat via `/ctx-status`, terminal via `ctxr`).\r","82820-\r","82821-**DO NOT build (adversarially-verified anti-patterns):**\r","--","82831-### Recommendations\r","82832-\r","82833:**1. Create `~/.claude/commands/ctx-status.md`** (user-level, available in every project):\r","82834-\r","82835-```markdown\r","--","82857-### Trade-off matrix\r","82858-\r","82859:| Path | Cost | Discoverability | \"Stay out of it\" | Fit |\r","82860-|---|---|---|---|---|\r","82861-| **Slash command `/ctx-status`** | 1 file, ~10 min | HIGH (`/help`) | PERFECT (only on demand) | ✅ Ship |\r","--","82885-\r","82886-## Related\r","82887:- [[projects/CTX/research/20260410-session-6c4f589e-chat-memory|20260410-session-6c4f589e-chat-memory]]\r","82888-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260402-project-understanding-evaluation-framework|20260402-project-understanding-evaluation-framework]]\r","82889-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260330-ctx-academic-critique-web-grounded|20260330-ctx-academic-critique-web-grounded]]\r","--","82890-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260327-ctx-real-project-self-eval|20260327-ctx-real-project-self-eval]]\r","--","82891-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260411-hook-comparison-auto-index-vs-chat-memory|20260411-hook-comparison-auto-index-vs-chat-memory]]\r","--","82892-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260408-g1-longterm-memory-evaluation-framework|20260408-g1-longterm-memory-evaluation-framework]]\r","--","82893-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260411-chat-memory-threshold-principled|20260411-chat-memory-threshold-principled]]\r","--","82894-- [[projects/CTX/decisions/20260326-path-derived-module-to-file|20260326-path-derived-module-to-file]]","--","82895-</file>","--","82896-","--","82897:<file path=\"docs/research/20260421-ctx-monetization-session-summary.md\">","82898-# CTX Monetization Session Summary — 2026-04-21\r","82899-\r","--","82900-**Session goal**: Turn the CTX dashboard into a monetizable product surface — optimize loading, eval proof score against monetization tiers, and validate the utility claim.\r","--","82901-\r","82902-**Status**: P1 loading optimization shipped. Utility proof at 32.5% LLM-judge validated (N=169, single-user). Further validation required before public pricing — see §6.\r","--","82903-\r","82904----\r","--","82905-\r","82906:## 1. What CTX actually does (production, verified)\r","82907-\r","82908-Single source of truth: `~/.claude/settings.json` `hooks` block (2026-04-19).\r","--","82910-| Hook | Event | Function | Status |\r","82911-|------|-------|----------|--------|\r","82912:| `chat-memory.py` | UserPromptSubmit | CM — vault.db FTS5 + vec0 hybrid (α=0.5 cosine + 0.5 bm25) | hybrid live (vec-daemon required) |\r","82913-| `bm25-memory.py --rich` | UserPromptSubmit | G1 decisions + G2-DOCS + G2-PREFETCH + G2-HOOKS unified | pure BM25, v3 tokenizer + T1-C router |\r","82914-| `memory-keyword-trigger.py` | UserPromptSubmit | Decision keyword detection → MEMORY.md write nudge | live |\r","--","82915-| `g2-fallback.py` | PostToolUse (Grep) | Grep miss → code-search MCP hint | live |\r","--","82916-| vec-daemon SessionStart guard | SessionStart | Real socket probe + stale-socket restart | live (fixed 2026-04-17) |\r","--","82917-| `utility-rate.py` | Stop | Per-turn substring + semantic utility scoring | live (async) |\r","82918-\r","--","82933-6. Other signals (pills)\r","82934-7. Knowledge graph (vis-network, lazy-loaded)\r","82935:8. Function proof — real samples (eye-check, per-prompt utility pills, load-more)\r","82936-9. Recent events (last 30)\r","82937-\r","--","82962-**Deferred (not P1)**:\r","82963-- P2: time-saved A/B telemetry — needs multi-day opt-in cohort, not shippable in one pass\r","82964:- P4: team-shared memory — product decision, not engineering\r","82965-\r","82966----\r","--","82967-\r","82968:## 4. Utility proof score (current evidence)\r","82969-\r","82970-**Pipeline**:\r","--","82971-- T0 substring match: 9.9% pooled rate (strict word-boundary on content tokens, meta-word filter)\r","82972:- T1 semantic rerank (e5-small, threshold 0.85): **38.8% pooled rate** — N=169 items across 7 projects, 1 user\r","82973-- T2 LLM-judge calibration (MiniMax M2.5, 3 parallel reviewers, batch-re-calibrated): **32.5% agreement rate** (55 Y / 114 N on a 169-sample pool)\r","82974-  - Precision 44.6%, Recall 81.8%, Agreement 60.9%\r","--","82979-- Fired 3× in the session (cross-session recall that user would have lost without CTX)\r","82980-\r","82981:**Honest summary**: ~1 in 3 injected items is actually used by Claude in its reply, measured on the Stop hook against the assistant's own output. Not marketing; reproducible from `~/.claude/ctx-telemetry.jsonl`.\r","82982-\r","82983----\r","--","82984-\r","82985:## 5. Monetization-tier fit (entity-crd eval)\r","82986-\r","82987-Applied the monetization corpus tier framework against current evidence:\r","--","82988-\r","82989-| Tier | Price | Gate | CTX current? |\r","--","82990-|------|-------|------|--------------|\r","82991-| Free | $0 | Any individual dev | ✅ ready — install script + hook files exist |\r","--","82992:| Commodity | $9–15/mo | Recurring value, low friction | ⚠️ price-ready, proof too thin for paid conversion |\r","82993-| Pro | $19–29/mo | Demonstrable time-saved or retention lift | ❌ time-saved A/B not run |\r","82994-| Team | $49–79/seat | Shared memory + admin | ❌ not built (P4, deferred) |\r","--","82995-| Enterprise | $200+ | SOC2 / on-prem / SLA | ❌ not built |\r","--","82996-\r","82997-**Gate to cross next tier**: Pro tier requires a time-saved A/B proxy (N ≥ 30 multi-user, 14-day opt-in cohort) — NOT a bigger T2 pool on the same single user.\r","--","82998-\r","82999----\r","--","83004-\r","83005-Short form:\r","83006:1. **N=1 user bias** — all 169 items are the founder's own usage across 7 projects. Utility rate on other users is **unmeasured**.\r","83007-2. **N too small for tight CI** — 169 samples at 95% CI gives ±7 pp on the 32.5% rate. The gap between Commodity and Pro pricing tiers is narrower than ±7 pp of confidence.\r","83008-3. **Utility ≠ value delivered** — \"Claude referenced the injected item\" does NOT prove \"user saved time\" or \"user would have failed without it.\" Need a counter-factual proxy.\r","--","83009-\r","--","83010-**Specific next-step validation plan**:\r","--","83011-- Recruit 3–5 external users → 14-day opt-in telemetry cohort → N ≥ 500 items\r","83012-- Van Westendorp PSM with ≥ 100 respondents for pricing floor/ceiling\r","--","83013-- Time-saved A/B: coin-flip CTX on/off per session for same user; diff median time-to-first-edit\r","83014-- Publish calibrated utility rate with CI, not a point estimate\r","--","83016----\r","83017-\r","83018:## 7.
