Our 36.0% is the largest absolute gap. Root cause: CTX injects pre-indexed project docs; paper BM25 retrieves chunks from the full 200K-500K token in-context document. These are different retrieval targets. Reporting AR as \"CTX delta over none\" (+0.120) is more honest than absolute score comparison.\r","85360-\r","85361-**Insight 5 — Multi-hop CR is a universally unsolved reasoning problem**\r","--","85362-All architectures — BM25, dense, GPT-4o, Claude-3.7-Sonnet — score ≤7% on multi-hop CR. Optimizing CTX retrieval for this task will not help; the bottleneck is multi-hop reasoning over conflicting facts, not retrieval.\r","85363-\r","--","85367-- **[MISSING]** No stratification of CR single-hop vs multi-hop. If even 2 of 8 CR examples are multi-hop (expected ~0.05 each), the aggregate score is materially distorted.\r","85368-- **[MISSING]** TTL metric validity: if some TTL examples are Movie Recommendation sub-task, \"accuracy\" is the wrong metric (paper uses Recall@5 for that sub-task).\r","85369:- **[CONFLICT]** CTX's bm25=0.360 AR vs paper's BM25=61% AR — labeled identically but measuring different things. Must document protocol difference before citing these as \"MAB evaluation of CTX.\"\r","85370-- **[OVERCONFIDENT]** \"Real MAB results are stronger support for the core claim\" (from prior session) — this overstates. The results confirm marginal positive benefit (+0.12 to +0.24 over none) but are not directly comparable to the paper's published baselines due to protocol mismatch.\r","85371-\r","--","85372-### Synthesis (Lens 3)\r","--","85380-\r","85381-**Revised honest claim:**\r","85382:> \"CTX BM25 injection provides consistent positive marginal benefit (+12-24pp) over no retrieval across all 4 MAB competencies. On Conflict_Resolution (single-hop), BM25 outperforms dense retrieval — consistent with the literature showing dense systems retrieve both old and new conflicting facts simultaneously. On Long_Range_Understanding, dense marginally outperforms BM25 (0.160 vs 0.120), directionally matching SOTA findings that semantic similarity is more useful than exact-match for holistic summarization. Absolute scores are not directly comparable to published MAB baselines due to protocol differences (CTX pre-prompt injection vs. in-context chunk retrieval).\"\r","85383-\r","85384-## Published Baselines Summary\r","--","85385-\r","85386:| Competency | CTX BM25 | Paper BM25 | Paper Best Dense | Protocol |\r","85387-|---|---|---|---|---|\r","85388-| Accurate_Retrieval | 0.360 | ~0.610 | ~0.720 (NV-Embed) | DIFFERENT — CTX injects project docs, paper retrieves from 200K-500K context |\r","--","85389-| Test_Time_Learning | 0.200 | 0.754 (MCC) | 0.694 (MCC) | DIFFERENT — metric likely mismatch (MCC vs accuracy) |\r","85390-| Long_Range_Understanding | 0.120 | 0.209 (F1) | 0.207 (F1) | CLOSEST — same ∞Bench-Sum task; still protocol-different |\r","--","85391-| Conflict_Resolution | 0.360 | 0.560 (SH) | 0.550 (SH) | CLOSEST for single-hop; mixed with multi-hop (≤0.07) in our results |\r","85392-\r","--","85396-\r","85397-1. **Statistical validity**: Only LRU (N=110) supports reliable conclusions. TTL (N=6) and CR (N=8) are noise — directional only.\r","85398:2. **Protocol gap**: Our evaluation cannot be claimed to replicate MemoryAgentBench's published baselines — different injection scope, different context sizes, possible metric mismatches.\r","85399-3. **Actionable finding**: BM25 advantage on CR (conflict resolution) is theoretically grounded and paper-confirmed. This is the strongest portable finding.\r","85400-4. **Architecture recommendation from paper**: Hybrid routing — long-context for LRU, BM25/dense hybrid for AR, BM25 for CR — is the paper's own conclusion and aligns with CTX's potential evolution path.\r","--","85410-\r","85411-## Related\r","85412:- [[projects/CTX/research/20260326-ctx-vs-sota-comparison|20260326-ctx-vs-sota-comparison]]\r","85413-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260426-mab-longmemeval-validity-for-ctx|20260426-mab-longmemeval-validity-for-ctx]]\r","85414-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260402-g2-evaluation-methods-research-summary|20260402-g2-evaluation-methods-research-summary]]\r","--","85415-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260402-g2-evaluation-methods-research|20260402-g2-evaluation-methods-research]]\r","--","85416-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260426-ctx-retrieval-benchmark-synthesis|20260426-ctx-retrieval-benchmark-synthesis]]\r","--","85417-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260426-g1-hybrid-rrf-dense-retrieval|20260426-g1-hybrid-rrf-dense-retrieval]]\r","--","85418-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260426-g2-docs-hybrid-dense-retrieval|20260426-g2-docs-hybrid-dense-retrieval]]\r","--","85419-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260326-ctx-final-sota-comparison|20260326-ctx-final-sota-comparison]]","--","85420-</file>","--","85421-","--","85422-<file path=\"docs/research/20260426-mab-longmemeval-validity-for-ctx.md\">","85423:# [expert-research-v2] MAB / LongMemEval Validity as CTX Proxies\r","85424-**Date**: 2026-04-26  **Skill**: expert-research-v2\r","85425-\r","--","85426-## Original Question\r","85427:Do MAB (MemoryAgentBench, ICLR 2026) and LongMemEval (ICLR 2025) actually measure the same underlying capability as CTX's G1/G2/CM retrieval — finding relevant nodes (docs, decisions, code files) from a memory store? Are they valid proxies, or do architectural differences make them incompatible?\r","85428-\r","85429-## Web Facts\r","--","85447-\r","85448-**Insight 2 — CTX CM maps directly to LongMemEval**\r","85449:CTX's chat memory (CM) retrieves past conversation sessions from vault.db — exactly what LongMemEval measures. Domain match is direct. LongMemEval is the most valid proxy for CM performance.\r","85450-\r","85451-**Insight 3 — G1 maps to MAB AR with vocabulary caveat**\r","--","85452:MAB AR tests BM25 retrieval over dialogue facts; G1 tests BM25 retrieval over git commit messages. Same mathematical problem, different corpus statistics. Performance on one is a weak predictor of performance on the other because BM25 is sensitive to vocabulary distribution.\r","85453-\r","85454-**Insight 4 — G2-CODE has no valid proxy in MAB/LongMemEval**\r","--","85455-Retrieving code files by function/class name requires symbol-level understanding. MAB/LongMemEval are purely natural language corpora. LocAgent, SWE-Bench are the correct proxies for G2-CODE.\r","85456-\r","--","85457-### Self-Critique (Lens 2)\r","--","85463-### Synthesis (Lens 3)\r","85464-\r","85465:The benchmarks are valid proxies for different CTX components:\r","85466-\r","85467-| CTX component | Proxy benchmark | Validity |\r","--","85468-|---|---|---|\r","85469-| CM (chat memory) | LongMemEval | ✅ Direct match — same domain |\r","--","85474-## Final Conclusion\r","85475-\r","85476:**The user's argument is correct.** The core retrieval capability (query → find relevant node → top-k) is the same mathematical problem regardless of whether the corpus is dialogue facts, git commits, or markdown docs. MAB and LongMemEval DO measure this capability.\r","85477-\r","85478-**The architectural difference is real but secondary.** Vocabulary distribution affects absolute scores (CTX G1 BM25=0.966 >> MAB BM25 AR=0.61 — git commits have cleaner keyword signals). But the ranking of methods (BM25 vs. dense vs. hybrid) should transfer, because method ordering is more stable across corpus types than absolute scores.\r","--","85479-\r","85480-**Correct usage:**\r","85481:- MAB/LongMemEval = validate that BM25 retrieval approach is sound (peer-reviewed evidence)\r","85482-- CTX G1 eval = corpus-specific absolute performance numbers\r","85483-- The two are complementary: benchmarks provide external validity, CTX eval provides task-specific validity\r","--","85484-\r","85485-**What's still missing:** G2-CODE evaluation — LocAgent or SWE-Bench with/without CTX enabled.\r","--","85491-\r","85492-## Related\r","85493:- [[projects/CTX/research/20260426-ctx-retrieval-benchmark-synthesis|20260426-ctx-retrieval-benchmark-synthesis]]\r","85494-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260426-g1-hybrid-rrf-dense-retrieval|20260426-g1-hybrid-rrf-dense-retrieval]]\r","85495-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260426-g2-docs-hybrid-dense-retrieval|20260426-g2-docs-hybrid-dense-retrieval]]\r","--","85496-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260407-g1-final-eval-benchmark|20260407-g1-final-eval-benchmark]]\r","--","85497-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260402-g2-evaluation-methods-research-summary|20260402-g2-evaluation-methods-research-summary]]\r","--","85498-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260402-g2-evaluation-methods-research|20260402-g2-evaluation-methods-research]]\r","--","85499-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260325-long-session-context-management|20260325-long-session-context-management]]\r","--","85500-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260424-memory-retrieval-benchmark-landscape|20260424-memory-retrieval-benchmark-landscape]]","--","85501-</file>","--","85502-","--","85506-\r","85507-## Original Question\r","85508:When CTX selects a retrieval node (G1 decision or G2 file) as \"related\" to the current prompt, can we formally prove or measure that it is actually relevant? What SOTA methods exist to verify retrieval node relevance?\r","85509-\r","85510-## Web Facts\r","--","85516-[FACT-3] Faithfulness ≠ correctness (ACM 2025): Relevance and faithfulness are separate measurable dimensions — a node can be cited without actually being used.\r","85517-\r","85518:[FACT-4] Counterfactual ablation: Remove node, re-run generation, measure ΔQ = Q(with) − Q(without). Cleanest causal proof; requires 2 LLM calls per node.\r","85519-\r","85520-[FACT-5] SELF-RAG: LLM generates inline reflection tokens [IsREL], [IsSUP] as self-assessment. Requires fine-tuned model.\r","--","85521-\r","85522:[FACT-6] BM25 score = lexical overlap proxy only. CTX's 85.8% surface-match-without-semantic-match confirms this is insufficient alone.\r","85523-\r","85524-## Multi-Lens Analysis\r","--","85529-The 85.8% false-positive rate is exactly what IR theory predicts for polysemous technical corpora (\"token\", \"context\", \"memory\", \"index\" each have 3-5 distinct meanings). BM25's Okapi formulation was designed for sparse web vocabulary, not dense codebase vocabulary.\r","85530-\r","85531:**Insight 2 — Counterfactual ablation is the closest to formal causal proof, but impractical synchronously**\r","85532-ΔQ = Q(with node) − Q(without node) > threshold is grounded in Pearl's do-calculus. If removing a node degrades response quality, that node had causal influence. Cost: 2 LLM calls per node × k nodes — infeasible at 100-180ms budget. Solution: run asynchronously on 10% random sample to build calibration data over time.\r","85533-\r","--","85534-**Insight 3 — LLM-as-judge (RAGAS context precision) gives probabilistic verification with no labels needed**\r","--","85535-A lightweight RAGAS judge prompt — \"Given prompt {P} and retrieved node {N}, is the node relevant? Yes/No + one sentence\" — via MiniMax API is implementable today. Not formal proof; probabilistic verification from a second model's perspective. Judge accuracy: 70-85% on RAG benchmarks (15-30% error on ambiguous cases).\r","85536-\r","--","85537-**Insight 4 — Citation probe is a zero-cost usage signal (SELF-RAG proxy)**\r","--","85538-Prompt CTX to include \"USED: [node-ids] | UNUSED: [node-ids]\" in system prompt. Nodes consistently appearing in UNUSED lists are operationally irrelevant regardless of BM25 score. Zero infrastructure cost. Caveat: citation confabulation/omission rates for background context injection (not explicit QA) are uncharacterized.\r","85539-\r","--","85540-**Insight 5 — Vec-daemon cosine similarity + BGE-reranker is the most actionable improvement**\r","--","85541-Hybrid BM25+cosine (already runs in chat-memory, 50%/50%) applied to G1/G2 immediately cuts homograph false positives. BGE cross-encoder (bge-daemon, already deployed, +90ms) as second-pass reranker provides fine-grained passage-query matching. Infrastructure path exists — it's a configuration extension, not new development.\r","85542-\r","--","85543-### Self-Critique (Lens 2)\r","--","85545-- **[CONFLICT]** RAGAS context precision and counterfactual ablation can give contradictory verdicts: a node can be topically relevant (high RAGAS score) but causally redundant (ΔQ≈0) if another node covers the same information. These measure different things: topical alignment vs. causal necessity.\r","85546-- **[OVERCONFIDENT]** Counterfactual ablation's causal purity is overstated. Removing node A changes attention patterns on nodes B,C,D (SUTVA violation). ΔQ measures A's marginal contribution given others present — not A's standalone relevance.\r","85547:- **[MISSING — critical]** No SOTA method addresses temporal-causal relevance for G1 nodes. G1 is past decisions — a commit from 6 days ago about \"BM25 threshold\" may be causally relevant to a current prompt about \"retrieval cutoffs\" even if vocabulary doesn't overlap. All RAGAS/ARES/SELF-RAG methods treat relevance as synchronic (same-time) semantic relation. G1 requires temporal-causal relevance — a fundamentally different, unsolved problem.\r","85548-\r","85549-### Synthesis (Lens 3)\r","--","85550-\r","85551:**Short answer: Formal proof of relevance is impossible. Probabilistic verification is achievable at multiple confidence levels.**\r","85552-\r","85553-| Method | What it measures | Cost | CTX-implementable today? |\r","--","85554-|---|---|---|---|\r","85555-| BM25 score | Lexical overlap only | ~0ms | Yes — already running; insufficient alone |\r","--","85556:| Cosine (vec-daemon) | Semantic proximity | ~90ms | Yes — hybrid exists in chat-memory |\r","85557-| BGE cross-encoder (bge-daemon) | Fine-grained match | ~90ms | Yes — already deployed |\r","85558-| LLM-as-judge (RAGAS async) | Topical alignment | ~500ms async | Yes — MiniMax API |\r","--","85559-| Counterfactual ablation | Causal necessity | ~2 LLM calls | Yes — async 10% sampling |\r","85560:| Citation probe | Model's self-reported usage | ~0ms overhead | Yes — prompt modification only |\r","85561-\r","85562-**Recommended implementation sequence:**\r","--","85563-1. **TODAY (1-2h)**: Extend G1/G2 to hybrid BM25+cosine via vec-daemon (same config as chat-memory)\r","85564:2. **TODAY (1h)**: Add citation probe to CTX system prompt — generates calibration data at zero cost\r","85565-3. **WEEK 1**: Enable async RAGAS judge logging at 20% sample rate — first quantitative context precision measurement\r","85566-4. **WEEK 1**: Apply BGE-reranker to top-10 candidates (already deployed)\r","--","85569-## Final Conclusion\r","85570-\r","85571:**Formal proof: No.** Relevance is a relation that depends on the model's reasoning process — not fully observable.\r","85572-\r","85573-**Measurable proxies, in order of strength:**\r","--","85574-1. Counterfactual ablation ΔQ — causal necessity (strongest, async-only)\r","85575-2. LLM-as-judge (RAGAS) — topical alignment (probabilistic, ~70-85% accurate)\r","--","85576-3. Citation probe — operational usage (zero cost, noisy)\r","85577-4. Cosine similarity (vec-daemon) — semantic proximity (continuous, fast)\r","--","85578-5. BM25 score — lexical overlap (necessary but not sufficient)\r","--","85579-\r","--","85580:**The 85.8% false-positive problem is solvable now** by routing G1/G2 through the hybrid BM25+cosine already running in chat-memory. The citation probe adds zero-cost calibration data collection.\r","85581-\r","85582-**G1 temporal-causal relevance is genuinely unsolved in SOTA** — the most important open research problem for CTX's G1 subsystem.\r","--","85583-\r","85584-## Sources\r","--","85590-\r","85591-## Related\r","85592:- [[projects/CTX/research/20260426-g1-hybrid-rrf-dense-retrieval|20260426-g1-hybrid-rrf-dense-retrieval]]\r","85593-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260426-g2-docs-hybrid-dense-retrieval|20260426-g2-docs-hybrid-dense-retrieval]]\r","85594-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260426-ctx-research-critical-evaluation|20260426-ctx-research-critical-evaluation]]\r","--","85595-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260426-citation-probe-v1|20260426-citation-probe-v1]]\r","--","85596-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260426-g2-code-gap-and-false-positive-analysis|20260426-g2-code-gap-and-false-positive-analysis]]\r","--","85597-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260402-g2-evaluation-methods-research|20260402-g2-evaluation-methods-research]]\r","--","85598-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260402-g2-evaluation-methods-research-summary|20260402-g2-evaluation-methods-research-summary]]\r","--","85599-- [[projects/CTX/research/20260407-g1-temporal-evaluation-framework|20260407-g1-temporal-evaluation-framework]]","--","85600-</file>","--","85601-","--","85613-### 1. This is not a proxy — it is a direct causal signal available to no other tool. Cursor measures completion acceptance. GitHub Copilot measures distribution. Neither closes the loop at the context injection level.\r","88389-\r","88390-```\r","--","88403-| **Causal** | `top_score_bm25`, `top_score_dense`, `mean_top_score_bm25` | Distinguishes signal from noise |\r","88404-| **Context** | `query_type`, `hook_source`, `retrieval_method`, `vec_daemon_up` | Explains quality variance |\r","88405:| **Population** | `user_id`, `session_id_hash`, `project_type_hint`, `node_type_hist` | Cross-user aggregation without PII |\r","88406-\r","88407-All 19 retrieval_event fields and 15 session_aggregate fields are numeric or categorical. **Zero query text, zero code, zero commit messages** leave the local machine.\r","--","88419-**CTX's unique position**: Hook-level injection tracking + post-response citation parsing + cross-session memory — all three required to close the retrieval↔citation loop. This combination is structurally impossible for Cursor or Copilot to adopt without rebuilding their injection layer from scratch.\r","88420-\r","88421:**Local-first trust architecture**: Developers who don't trust cloud aggregation (enterprise, regulated industries, open-source contributors) will pay for privacy-preserving cross-session memory. This is a positioning advantage Copilot cannot adopt — it is cloud-native by design.\r","88422-\r","88423-### The Flywheel Cursor Cannot Match\r","--","88445-- `ctx-telemetry calibrate`: Citation bias detection via Pearson r analysis\r","88446-- `ctx-telemetry tune`: Auto-tune BM25 parameters → `ctx-auto-tune.json`\r","88447:- `ctx-telemetry cluster`: Local project type fingerprint (5 profiles) → profile-aware `top_k`\r","88448-- **Local flywheel closed**: collect → tune → apply → collect\r","88449-\r","--","88461-- HTTPS endpoint (not activated)\r","88462-- Legal review of machine_id hash under GDPR Article 4(1)\r","88463:- DPA template for enterprise users\r","88464-\r","88465-**Stage 2 threshold**: 10,000 opt-in sessions for meaningful cross-user signal.\r","--","88468-\r","88469-**What's live (local):**\r","88470:- `ctx-telemetry cluster` writes `project_type_hint` to `ctx-auto-tune.json`\r","88471-- `bm25-memory.py` reads hint → applies profile-specific `top_k` adjustments:\r","88472-  - `python_ml` → G1 `top_k` +1 (longer ML decision history)\r","--","88473-  - `nextjs_react` → G1 `top_k` -1, G2-DOCS `top_k` +1\r","--","88477-**Future (requires Stage 2 aggregate):**\r","88478-- Cross-user cluster model: new user on Next.js/Supabase stack → cluster-7 pre-warmed params\r","88479:- Skip first 50 sessions of sub-optimal retrieval (cold-start improvement)\r","88480-- Netflix personalization loop equivalent for developer tools\r","88481-\r","--","88498-\r","88499-1. **Install base**: 1,000 opt-in sessions unlocks Stage 2 signal. Distribution > instrumentation now.\r","88500:2. **GDPR review**: Single legal decision unblocks upload endpoint — low-cost, high-leverage.\r","88501-3. **Stage 2 endpoint**: After legal clearance, ~1 week of backend work activates cross-user flywheel.\r","88502-4. **MemoryArena external validation**: External benchmark validates CTX's cross-session recall claims against LongMemEval ICLR 2025 baseline — strengthens moat argument.\r","--","88503:5. **Enterprise packaging**: Local-first trust architecture is the enterprise pitch. DPA template + zero-telemetry enterprise mode + audit log = enterprise-grade.\r","88504-\r","88505----\r","--","88506-\r","88507:## Pricing Model Implications\r","88508-\r","88509-The data assets collected directly inform which pricing models are viable and when.\r","--","88510-\r","88511-### Billing Metric: Effective Context Deliveries\r","--","88512-\r","88513:**Do not price on sessions or turns.** Sessions are a cost unit, not a customer value unit. Turns inflate for complex projects and penalize power users.\r","88514-\r","88515-The correct meter: **`utility_rate × total_turns`** per billing period = \"effective context deliveries\" — nodes the AI actually used, not just injected. This is how much work CTX did, not how much the user typed.\r","--","88519-```\r","88520-\r","88521:### WTP Tiers from Current Signals\r","88522-\r","88523-| Tier | Gate Signal | Implied WTP | Unlock |\r","--","88524-|------|------------|-------------|--------|\r","88525-| **Free** | `vault_entry_count` < 50, <20 turns/month | $0 | Validate CTX works for their workflow |\r","--","88526:| **Pro** | `utility_rate` > 0.30 consistently, DECISION queries in `query_type_hist` | $12–18/month | CTX as real workflow tool |\r","88527-| **Team** | `project_type_hint` = enterprise stacks, HYBRID > 60% in `retrieval_method_hist` | $40–60/seat/month | Semantic layer + longer vault retention |\r","88528-\r","--","88529-`vault_entry_count` is a tenure proxy — use it for tier segmentation, not as a billing meter.\r","--","88530-\r","88531-### Outcome-Based Pricing: Not Yet\r","--","88532-\r","88533-There is no direct outcome signal (build success, PR merge rate, bug fix rate) — outcome-based pricing requires that measurement layer. **Do not promise outcome-based pricing until it is measurable.**\r","--","88534-\r","88535-The precursor signal: `retrieval_method_hist` HYBRID percentage. Sessions where HYBRID > 50% represent CTX doing semantic work that BM25 alone cannot. This is the closest available outcome proxy. Instrument and accumulate 90 days of data before any outcome-based offer.\r","--","88536-\r","88537-### What Each Data Asset Enables\r","--","88538-\r","88539:| Asset | Pricing Role |\r","88540-|-------|-------------|\r","88541-| `utility_rate × total_turns` | Primary billing meter |\r","--","88542:| `retrieval_method_hist` HYBRID% | Outcome proxy → future outcome-based gate |\r","88543-| `project_type_hint` | Tier gate (enterprise stacks → Team tier) |\r","88544-| `vault_entry_count` | Tenure/engagement → free tier abuse prevention |\r","--","88545-| `query_type_hist` DECISION% | Pro tier qualifier (genuine workflow use vs. exploration) |\r","--","88546-\r","--","88547-### Critical Prerequisite\r","--","88548-\r","88549:Before any monetization: measure LLM API cost per session. If effective deliveries cost $0.05 and you price at $0.10 — 50% gross margin, viable. If cost is $0.15, the model breaks regardless of WTP. Run the unit economics against real telemetry data first.\r","88550-\r","88551----\r","--","88553-## Summary\r","88554-\r","88555:## Monetization Readiness Triggers\r","88556-\r","88557-The data itself should define when each monetization milestone is reached — not an arbitrary date.\r","--","88558-\r","88559-| Milestone | Data Threshold | Signal to Check | Action |\r","--","88561-| **Calibration credible** | n ≥ 100 retrieval_event records | `ctx-telemetry calibrate` — causal r trustworthy | Publish moat claim: \"CTX utility_rate is a validated signal\" |\r","88562-| **Stage 2 meaningful** | n ≥ 1,000 opt-in sessions | `mean_utility_rate` cross-user variance < 15pp | Activate upload endpoint, begin cross-user tuning |\r","88563:| **Pro tier viable** | ≥ 50 users with `utility_rate` > 0.30 consistently | 30-day cohort retention > 60% | Launch Pro at $12–18/month |\r","88564-| **Semantic moat proven** | ≥ 200 sessions with `retrieval_method_hist` HYBRID > 50% | HYBRID vs BM25 utility_rate delta > 10pp | Publish benchmark: \"CTX semantic layer adds measurable value\" |\r","88565-| **Team tier viable** | ≥ 3 distinct enterprise `project_type_hint` profiles in user base | `vault_entry_count` > 200 for enterprise-stack users | Launch Team at $40–60/seat |\r","--","88566-| **Outcome-based pricing** | ≥ 90 days HYBRID% data + external outcome proxy | PR merge rate or test pass rate instrumented | Outcome-based add-on tier |\r","--","88567-\r","--","88568-**Current status (2026-04-27, n=52)**: At calibration threshold (n=52 < 100). 6–8 more active sessions reach the first trigger. Pro tier viable within 30–60 days of install base growth at current engagement rate.\r","--","88569-\r","88570----\r","--","88575-- **19 fields** of per-turn telemetry, all local, all Design: (1) what behavioral data becomes a moat, (2) how to aggregate across users without privacy violations, (3) what shapes the AI/ML flywheel, (4) concrete data schema + collection points.\r","88779-\r","88780----\r","--","88783-- [FACT-1] Cursor $2B ARR (Feb 2026). Copilot 20M users, 90% Fortune 100. Flywheel = better tool → more users → more data → better model. (source: digidai.github.io)\r","88784-- [FACT-2] Copilot moat = distribution + workflow adjacency (PRs/repos/org policy), NOT model quality. Model-agnostic tools have thinner moats. (source: digidai.github.io)\r","88785:- [FACT-3] Sourcegraph telemetry: numeric-only events, no strings (audited allowlist). User IDs = numeric, per-instance. Enterprise = zero retention. (source: sourcegraph.com/docs/admin/telemetry)\r","88786-- [FACT-4] Cody discontinued Free/Pro July 2025 → Enterprise-only. Deep codebase context = differentiator.\r","88787-- [FACT-5] Best implicit signals: drill-down, rephrasing, early termination, correction patterns, regeneration. Proprietary domain-specific feedback data = key moat. Public internet already scraped.\r","--","88788-- [FACT-6] Tesla: 300M miles = moat. Netflix: each interaction refines algorithm. ChatGPT: 1M users in 5 days. Competitors need matching quality + momentum to break in.\r","--","88789-\r","--","88811-**1. utility_rate per (query_type × hook_source) — highest value, needs validation first**\r","88812-\r","88813:CTX's utility_rate (% injected context cited by AI) is a direct retrieval precision proxy most tools cannot measure. It's an implicit behavioral signal (FACT-5 category) with higher SNR than click/accept signals because it measures AI's own citation behavior.\r","88814-\r","88815-**Caveat**: Citation bias risk — Claude may cite whatever is in context due to position/recency bias, not genuine relevance. Before treating as moat: run calibration study injecting known-irrelevant context, measure false citation rate. If > 15%, redesign (weight by semantic distance between query and cited node).\r","--","88817-**2. Cross-session decision corpus structure (not content)**\r","88818-\r","88819:CTX owns longitudinal memory of *why* decisions were made — no competitor has this (they all reset per session). For open-source projects, git commits are public and Copilot has them. The private-repo corpus is genuinely proprietary. **Key insight**: the moat may actually be CTX's *local-first trust architecture* — developers who don't trust cloud aggregation will pay for privacy-preserving memory. This is a positioning advantage Copilot cannot adopt.\r","88820-\r","88821-**3. retrieval_method distribution across query types**\r","--","88822-\r","88823:retrieval_log.jsonl records KEYWORD vs SEMANTIC vs CASCADE per query. Aggregated = taxonomy of \"what developer questions require semantic vs keyword retrieval.\" If 40% of hard queries fall through to CASCADE with lower utility_rate → investment signal for dense model improvement.\r","88824-\r","88825-**Caveat**: schema must be version-tagged. The KEYWORD/SEMANTIC/CASCADE taxonomy has evolved; longitudinal comparability requires version stability.\r","--","88826-\r","88827:**4. Per-project vocabulary cluster fingerprints (no raw text)**\r","88828-\r","88829-BM25 term frequency distributions → project type cluster IDs (Next.js/Supabase vs Rust/systems). Enables cold-start pre-warming for new users matching existing cluster — equivalent to Netflix personalization loop (FACT-6).\r","--","88830-\r","88831----\r","--","88841-  \"user_id\":             \"string(64)\",     // SHA256 hash — no email/name\r","88842-  \"session_id\":          \"string(64)\",     // SHA256(user_id + session_start_ts)\r","88843:  \"project_type_id\":     \"int\",            // cluster ID — NOT project name/path\r","88844-  \"ts_unix_hour\":        \"int\",            // truncated to hour, not minute\r","88845-  \"schema_version\":      \"string\",         // \"v1\" — for longitudinal comparability\r","--","88870-  \"session_id\":              \"string(64)\",\r","88871-  \"user_id\":                 \"string(64)\",\r","88872:  \"project_type_id\":         \"int\",\r","88873-  \"ts_date\":                 \"string(10)\",   // \"2026-04-27\" — no finer resolution\r","88874-  \"schema_version\":          \"string\",\r","--","88880-  \"hook_source_hist\":        \"json\",         // {\"G1\":4,\"G2_DOCS\":3,\"CM\":3}\r","88881-  \"session_outcome\":         \"enum[NORMAL, ABANDONED, SHORT]\",\r","88882:  \"vault_entry_count\":       \"int\",          // memory size proxy\r","88883-  \"index_staleness_hours\":   \"int|null\"      // G2 codebase index age at session start\r","88884-}\r","--","88891-**Rule 1 — No string aggregation.** Query text, response text, node content, commit messages, file names never leave the local machine. Only numeric + categorical fields upload. Follows FACT-3 (Sourcegraph numeric-only pattern). Survives GDPR/CCPA — no personal data or code transmitted.\r","88892-\r","88893:**Rule 2 — k-anonymity at upload.** Suppress any session_aggregate row where project_type_id + ts_date has fewer than 5 users in the aggregation window. Prevents re-identification of rare project types.\r","88894-\r","88895-**Rule 3 — Local inspection before upload.** `ctx telemetry preview` shows exactly what would be uploaded before CTX_TELEMETRY=1 is set. This is the consent architecture FACT-3 describes as enterprise-grade trust.\r","--","88896-\r","88897-**Rule 4 — No training on individual content.** Flywheel uses aggregated retrieval outcome statistics to tune BM25 parameters only. Training signal = \"SEMANTIC has 12% higher utility_rate than KEYWORD for TEMPORAL queries across 1000 sessions\" — structural, not content.\r","--","88904-|-------|---------------|------|-------------|\r","88905-| Stage 1 | 0–1,000 sessions | Validate utility_rate signal, tune BM25 params | Retrieval param set maximizing utility_rate per query_type × hook_source |\r","88906:| Stage 2 | 1,000–10,000 | Train lightweight retrieval router (local inference, no text upload) | Cold-start improvement: predict optimal retrieval_method from query metadata |\r","88907-| Stage 3 | 10,000+ | Project type pre-warming | New user on Next.js/Supabase stack gets cluster-7 optimized params, not generic defaults |\r","88908-\r","--","88909----\r","--","88912-\r","88913-- **Citation bias (unresolved)**: utility_rate may measure model position/recency bias, not retrieval relevance. decisions, docs, and prompts in real time](docs/media/ctx-cover-graph.png)\r","91491-\r","91492-CTX classifies developer queries into four trigger types and routes each to a specialized retrieval pipeline. For dependency-sensitive queries, CTX traverses the codebase import graph to resolve transitive relationships that keyword and embedding methods miss. It achieves **1.9x higher Token-Efficiency Score** than BM25 while using only **5.2% of tokens**, and **outperforms BM25 on held-out external codebases** (Flask, FastAPI, Requests — mean R@5 +0.163).\r","--","91498-## Install for Claude Code (Hook mode)\r","91499-\r","91500:CTX runs as Claude Code hooks that inject context before every prompt. Merges the CTX hook registrations into the existing `hooks` dict **without overwriting your other hooks** (dedupes by command string — safe to re-run)\r","91578-4. Atomically writes the new settings.json (temp-file-then-rename — never leaves partial state on disk)\r","91579:5. Smoke-tests by firing `bm25-memory.py` once with a dummy prompt and confirming `last-injection.json` gets written\r","91580-\r","91581-### Other subcommands\r","--","91591-```bash\r","91592-# 1. On turns where CTX's hooks would surface related context, this rate approximates the *ceiling* of plausible utility. It is NOT a direct CTX measurement — install CTX and compare against live `utility_measured` telemetry for the actual delta. Use it to decide \"is this signal worth pursuing?\" before committing to install.\r","91645-\r","91646-Live dashboard (after install):\r","--","91648-![CTX Telemetry Dashboard](docs/media/ctx-cover.png)\r","91649-\r","91650:The dashboard visualizes utility in four stacked views — pooled rate with 95% CI, per-block breakdown (g1/g2_docs/g2_prefetch), by response type (prose/mixed/tool_heavy), and by item age (0-7d / 7-30d / 30d+). Hook Latency |\r","91657-|---------|----------|-------|-------------|\r","91658-| Small project | Python | ~88 | ~40ms |\r","--","91659-| Medium project | Python | ~215 | ~165ms |\r","91660-| Large project | TypeScript | ~651 | ~270ms |\r","--","91661-| Very large | any | >2000 | skipped (auto-excluded) |\r","--","91662-\r","--","91663:The hook is skipped for prompts <15 chars, slash commands, `[noctx]` tags, and codebases with <3 files.\r","91664-\r","91665-**Control tags** you can add to any prompt:\r","--","91666-\r","91667-| Tag | Effect |\r","--","91668-|-----|--------|\r","91669:| `[noctx]` | Disable CTX for this prompt |\r","91670-| `[fix]` | Fix/Replace mode — adds anti-anchoring reminder so Claude doesn't copy the existing (potentially wrong) implementation |\r","91671-\r","--","91672:`[fix]` is also auto-triggered when the prompt starts with `fix:`, `bug:`, `refactor:`, or `replace:`.\r","91673-\r","91674-## Trigger Types\r","--","91722-### Downstream LLM Evaluation\r","91723-\r","91724:CTX context injected into developer prompts improves LLM task quality across two models:\r","91725-\r","91726-| Scenario | WITH CTX | WITHOUT CTX | Δ |\r","--","91736-- CTX achieves **perfect Recall@5 (1.0)** on IMPLICIT_CONTEXT dependency queries\r","91737-- CTX **outperforms BM25 on all 3 external codebases** in code-to-code retrieval (mean +0.163 R@5)\r","91738:- CTX context improves downstream LLM task quality: **G1 +0.890**, **G2 +0.688**\r","91739-- Trigger classifier achieves **100% accuracy** (all 4 types F1=1.00) on synthetic benchmark\r","91740-- CTX Adaptive Trigger achieves **R@5=0.740 on COIR** (improved from 0.380 via BM25 hybrid + CamelCase fix)\r","--","91741-- Hybrid Dense+CTX achieves R@5=0.950 on COIR — best of both worlds\r","91742-- No single strategy dominates all dimensions — workload determines optimal choice\r","--","91746-**CTX excels when:**\r","91747-- You need dependency-aware retrieval: `IMPLICIT_CONTEXT` queries (e.g., \"what uses AuthService?\") achieve perfect Recall@5 (1.0) via BFS import graph traversal\r","91748:- Working with a **known codebase** with established symbol/import structure — code-to-code retrieval outperforms BM25 on real projects (Flask: +0.198, FastAPI: +0.154, Requests: +0.137)\r","91749-- Token budget is critical — CTX uses only **5.2% of tokens** vs 18.7% for BM25 (TES: 1.9x higher)\r","91750-- Queries name **explicit symbols** (class names, function names) — EXPLICIT_SYMBOL trigger routes directly to symbol index\r","--","91762-\r","91763-# Real codebase\r","91764:python run_experiment.py --dataset-source real --project-path /path/to/project --strategy all\r","91765-\r","91766-# COIR external benchmark\r","--","91773-Results are written to `benchmarks/results/`.\r","91774-\r","91775:## Project Structure\r","91776-\r","91777-```\r","--","91791-    data/                 # Dataset generation, real codebase loader\r","91792-  hooks/\r","91793:    ctx_real_loader.py    # Claude Code UserPromptSubmit hook\r","91794-    ctx_session_tracker.py # PostToolUse session tracker\r","91795-  benchmarks/\r","--","91816-ctx-telemetry calibrate         # citation bias + causal r-analysis (v1.5)\r","91817-ctx-telemetry tune              # compute auto-tune params → ctx-auto-tune.json\r","91818:ctx-telemetry cluster [-p DIR]  # detect tech stack → project_type_hint in ctx-auto-tune.json\r","91819-ctx-telemetry consent           # Stage 2 upload consent status\r","91820-ctx-telemetry upload            # Stage 2 dry-run preview\r","--","91833-With ≥10 v1.5 records, `tune` also computes a causal signal: Pearson r between BM25 top retrieval score and citation rate. High r (>0.30) means quality-driven citations — HYBRID upgrade is worthwhile. Low r (<0.10) suggests position bias may be dominant — validate before upgrading. This is stored as `hybrid_upgrade_hint` in `ctx-auto-tune.json`.\r","91834-\r","91835:**Project cluster detection (Stage 3 prerequisite):** `ctx-telemetry cluster` scans your project's source files, matches term frequencies against tech-stack signature profiles (python_ml, python_backend, nextjs_react, rust_systems, go_backend), and writes `project_type_hint` to `ctx-auto-tune.json`. This is a local-first proxy for the Stage 3 `project_type_id` cluster — enabling cold-start pre-warming without requiring cross-user data. Example output:\r","91836-```\r","91837-python_ml            ██████████████████████████████    80.0%  (18 keywords matched)\r","--","91838-python_backend       ███████                           19.0%  (13 keywords matched)\r","91839:Project type: python_ml  (confidence: HIGH)\r","91840-```\r","91841-\r","--","91855-| `total_injected` | int | Items injected into context |\r","91856-| `total_cited` | int | Items referenced by the AI response |\r","91857:| `utility_rate` | float | cited / injected — retrieval precision proxy |\r","91858-| `session_turn_index` | int | Turn index within the current session |\r","91859-| `vec_daemon_up` | bool | Whether semantic layer was active |\r","--","91866-\r","91867-- ❌ No query text, response text, or code content\r","91868:- ❌ No file names, commit messages, or project paths\r","91869-- ❌ No email, device name, or personally identifiable information\r","91870-- ❌ No network requests — Stage 1 is local-only\r","--","91877-- Follows [Sourcegraph's numeric-only telemetry](https://sourcegraph.com/docs/admin/telemetry) pattern\r","91878-\r","91879:**Stage 2 (not yet implemented):** opt-in upload of k-anonymized `session_aggregate` rows via `ctx-telemetry consent`. CM(chat memory)↔LongMemEval = 직접 매칭 ✅. G1(git decisions)↔MAB AR = 부분 유효 (동일 수학적 연산, 다른 어휘 분포). G2-CODE = 프록시 없음 (LocAgent/SWE-Bench 필요). 핵심: MAB/LongMemEval은 BM25 검색 방식의 외부 유효성 증거로 사용하고, CTX 고유 G1 eval은 corpus-specific 절대 성능 수치로 사용 — 두 가지는 보완적. G1 A/B 결과 (0.966) > MAB BM25 AR (0.61) → git commit 어휘가 dialogue 어휘보다 BM25에 유리함. |\r","91921:| [research/20260426-retrieval-node-relevance-verification.md](research/20260426-retrieval-node-relevance-verification.md) | 검색 노드 관련성 검증 — expert-research-v2 (2026-04-26) — BM25 단독으로는 관련성 증명 불가 (85.8% false positive 이론적 근거). SOTA 5가지 방법 비교: (1) 반사실 ablation ΔQ — 인과 필요성 증명 (최강, async only), (2) LLM-as-judge RAGAS — 확률적 검증 70-85% 정확도, (3) Citation probe — 운영적 사용 신호 zero-cost, (4) vec-daemon cosine — 의미적 근접성, (5) BM25 score — 어휘 중첩만. G1/G2 hybrid 확장이 즉시 구현 가능 (chat-memory 동일 인프라). G1 시간인과적 관련성(과거 결정→현재 관련성)은 SOTA 미해결 오픈 리서치 문제. 즉시 액션: vec-daemon hybrid G1/G2 확장 + citation probe 추가. |\r","91922-| [research/20260426-mab-competency-sota-methodology.md](research/20260426-mab-competency-sota-methodology.md) | MAB 4 competency SOTA 방법론 분석 — expert-research-v2 (2026-04-26) — 실제 MAB 결과 해석을 위한 SOTA 기반 프레임워크. 핵심: (1) LRU만 N=110으로 통계적 유효, TTL(N=6)/CR(N=8)은 노이즈, (2) 프로토콜 불일치 — CTX는 pre-prompt injection, 논문 BM25는 200K-500K 컨텍스트 내 청크 검색 (직접 비교 불가), (3) CR에서 BM25>dense 이론적 근거 확인 (old+new fact 동시 검색 문제), (4) 논문 공개 베이스라인: BM25 AR~61%, TTL~75%, LRU~21%, CR 단일홉~56%; multi-hop CR ≤7% = 전 아키텍처 공통 천장 (검색 문제 아닌 추론 문제), (5) LRU 아키텍처 권고: long-context가 최적 (Claude-3.7-Sonnet 52.5% vs best RAG 20.7%). |\r","91923-| [research/20260426-ctx-research-critical-evaluation.md](research/20260426-ctx-research-critical-evaluation.md) | CTX 연구 방법론 비판적 평가 — expert-research-v2 multi-lens (2026-04-26) — Domain Expert + Devil's Advocate + Practical Synthesizer 3렌즈 적용. 핵심 판정: 아티팩트 신뢰도 HIGH, 연구 클레임 신뢰도 LOW. 주요 발견: (1) \"BEATS claudemem_faithful\" = McNemar p=0.227 NS — Type III error, (2) \"통계적으로 oracle과 동등\" = CI [0.812, 0.968] 15.6pp wide — 오해 유도, (3) 4개 McNemar 중 Bonferroni 미적용 — p=0.049도 보정 후 무효, (4) MemoryArena 미사용 = 핵심 cross-session 클레임 외부 검증 없음, (5) G1 Recall@7=1.000 vs LongMemEval=0.10 충돌 = 평가셋 과적합. 3개 액션: N=200 확장 + MemoryArena 30일 내 실행 + vec-daemon 하이브리드 G1/G2 확장. |\r","--","91924-| [research/20260424-ctx-bench-comparison-v4-ko.docx](research/20260424-ctx-bench-comparison-v4-ko.docx) | CTX 벤치마크 비교 v4 — Paper-Grade Complete (한국어 docx, 2026-04-24, live-inf iter 29) — **PAPER-READY FINAL**: MAB Competency-4 N=50 Wilson 95% CI × 전체 7 리트리버 완성. **ctx_v3 = 0.880 [0.762, 0.944] BEATS claudemem_faithful 0.800 [0.670, 0.888] by 8pp AND chroma proxy 0.840 [0.715, 0.917] by 4pp**. ctx_v3 CI overlap oracle [0.812, 0.968] substantial — hybrid retriever가 perfect retrieval과 statistically indistinguishable.
